name: Mizuki & Caerula Arbor
line:
The sea breeze blows past your shoulders to the other end of the ocean.
There lies your destination, but you are yet to begin your journey.
Pack your belongings and get ready, for the sea awaits your arrival.

zones.name: Sunshine Coast
zones.desc: The waves pass the steps devoid of footprints, the foam soaking the bricks at the base of the wall. This is a form of clamorous silence, where the land and the sea join in harmony.

zones.name: Offshore Island
zones.desc: This island is not far from shore, but nobody knows how it came to be. The ocean wind pushes ajar the doors of the buildings here, light and shadow interweaving... Go on—enter, and take a look.

zones.name: Raging Billows
zones.desc: The sky darkens, those dangerously beautiful tides shimmering as they gradually become clearer. They quietly spread towards the mouth of the cave on the island, right at our feet.

zones.name: Secret Hotbed
zones.desc: Deeper inside the cave, the moss creeping along the rock walls begins to proliferate wildly, covering the entire surface. Life does not require the light of day to thrive.

zones.name: Sacrosanct Cave
zones.desc: The ocean current abruptly veers here, the sound of the rushing tide resembling that of crying. They seem to have been deliberately avoiding this place... Apparently, nature is not the only inhabitant here.

zones.name: Deep Sea Woodland
zones.desc: The sound of plants breathing resonates at the same frequency, and countless motes of fluorescent light rise amidst the forest like a galaxy hanging upside-down on the ocean floor. What kind of soil could give rise to such a wondrous grove?

zones.name: Indigo Cradle
zones.desc: Time. Time. Time is their sustenance. Across lengths of time immeasurable, the cerulean branches continue to linger and grow, and thus does life sway and unfurl. This place has everything, and nurtures all.

nodeTypes.name: Combat Ops
nodeTypes.desc: Everyone has their weapons drawn. They're definitely not here to negotiate.
nodeTypes.func: A standard combat node. Win to earn collectibles, Originium Ingots, and other rewards.

nodeTypes.name: Emergency Ops
nodeTypes.desc: Some violence may well exceed your expectations, a very common occurrence.
nodeTypes.func: A harder fight with stronger enemies than regular Combat Ops — and better rewards (Hope, etc.) to match.

nodeTypes.name: Dreadful Foe
nodeTypes.desc: Criminals, ruffians, and the damned… You're in trouble.
nodeTypes.func: The boss battle guarding the end of each floor. You must clear it to move on to the next floor.

nodeTypes.name: Safe House
nodeTypes.desc: Nobody will bother you here, and there's plenty of food and water. No need to be so tense all the time.
nodeTypes.func: A rest node offering upkeep options such as restoring Objective HP or temporarily promoting Operators.

nodeTypes.name: Encounter
nodeTypes.desc: New friends, old rivals… Strange encounters never age. Only scars stand the test of time.
nodeTypes.func: A random-event node. Depending on your choices you gain rewards — or pay a price.

nodeTypes.name: Downtime Recreation
nodeTypes.desc: A little indulgence in life's simpler pleasures goes a long way in fighting the monotonous and the harrowing on your long road ahead.
nodeTypes.func: A recreation node with theme-specific minigames and wagers. Rewards depend on the outcome.

nodeTypes.name: Dense Fog
nodeTypes.desc: There's something in the mist, but you cannot see anything...
nodeTypes.func: An unidentified node hidden from view. Its true type is revealed when you enter.

nodeTypes.name: Wish Fulfilled
nodeTypes.desc: There are many ways of making a wish, but the wish itself is almost always the same thing.
nodeTypes.func: A node where you pick one recruitment voucher from a random selection.

nodeTypes.name: Fortuitous Opportunity
nodeTypes.desc: The lighthouse has fallen. People open their bags, delivering light for each other.
nodeTypes.func: A node where you trade what you have (Objective HP, collectibles, etc.) for other rewards.

nodeTypes.name: Emergency Dispatch
nodeTypes.desc: A lantern cannot illuminate without oil, a fincatching boat cannot sail without fuel. Do not underestimate the importance of adequate supplies.
nodeTypes.func: A node that sends some of your Operators on a detached mission. They leave your squad for a while and bring back rewards on return.

nodeTypes.name: Rogue Trader
nodeTypes.desc: Neither an ally nor an enemy. Some people like to sit on the fence.
nodeTypes.func: A shop where you spend Originium Ingots on collectibles and items, or restore Objective HP.

nodeTypes.name: Wander into Wonderland
nodeTypes.desc: And thus the bard sings: 'Oft are desolate fields and floral gardens neighbors; oft is losing one's way but another adventure.'
nodeTypes.func: An entrance to a special area. Entering leads to a separate zone or event.

nodeTypes.name: Regional Commissions
nodeTypes.desc: The long road is unfinished; the only ending you see is a new signpost.
nodeTypes.func: A node where you accept a commission and earn rewards for meeting its conditions.

difficulties.rule: 'Silent Squad' will escort you the whole way

difficulties.rule: Dive deeper into the ocean to face harsher trials

difficulties.rule: Starting Light -15

difficulties.rule: All enemies gain +15% Movement Speed

difficulties.name: Surging Waves
difficulties.rule: Increases Elemental Injury taken by 15%

difficulties.rule: All enemies gain +15 ASPD

difficulties.rule: Starting Light -15

difficulties.name: Surging Waves
difficulties.rule: All Elite and Boss units gain +20% HP

difficulties.name: Surging Waves
difficulties.rule: All enemies gain +10 RES

difficulties.name: Surging Waves
difficulties.rule: Increases Elemental Injury taken by 15%

difficulties.name: Surging Waves
difficulties.rule: Deployment Limit -1

difficulties.name: Surging Waves
difficulties.rule: All Elite and Boss units gain an additional +20% ATK, DEF, and HP

difficulties.name: Surging Waves
difficulties.rule: Received Collectible [Fosterer Genotype]

difficulties.name: Surging Waves
difficulties.rule: Composite Rejection will replace Basic Rejection

difficulties.name: Surging Waves
difficulties.rule: For the first 20 seconds after they appear, Elite and Leader enemies take 50% less Physical and Arts damage and have +30% ATK

difficulties.name: Memory Mapping
difficulties.rule: Monthly squads are refreshed on the first day of each month

difficulties.name: Deep Dive Mode
difficulties.rule: A challenge mode that provides a unique experience.

stages.name: Cistern
stages.desc:
The scramble for fresh water highlights what once was a matter of trust. When water is no longer safe to drink, panic rips through the masses. 
This stage has  that cause enemies inside to have reduced Movement Speed and continuously lose HP.

stages.name: Insect Infestation
stages.desc: Apparently, insect swarms are better at detecting danger than humans, so what are these bugs trying to avoid?

stages.name: Sniper Squad
stages.desc: Some archers have banded together to hunt along the coast. All living things are their prey.

stages.name: Symbiosis
stages.desc: As long as it is conducive to the survival of the collective, we many sometimes allow non-Seaborn to coexist with the colony, even providing aid when necessary.

stages.name: Mutual Aid
stages.desc: When the order of the world is broken, only the simplest way of telling friend from foe is available—those who stand with you are friends, and those who stand far away are foes.

stages.name: Voiceless Summons
stages.desc:
What is it that allows some humans to utilize Sea Terrors? Or, is this utilization nothing more than wishful thinking on the part of the humans? 
This stage has  that prevent your units inside from being targeted by enemy ranged attacks.

stages.name: Leviathan's Followers
stages.desc:
Some Church of the Deep cultists have heard of a Leviathan that floats on the surface of the ocean like an iceberg, so naturally they worship it as an avatar of ice and snow. But naturally, they have no idea what 'ice and snow' truly are, considering they've never seen it before. 
This stage has  that periodically apply the Cold status in an area around them.

stages.name: Malady
stages.desc:
The land's malady cannot infect the ocean, but the islands above the water's surface are still its playthings. 
This stage has  that periodically damage friendly and enemy units around them.

stages.name: Omen
stages.desc: The Church of the Deep cultists have brought a Sea Terror following its reproductive instincts ashore. If not dealt with promptly, there will be a small tide of Sea Terrors in the surrounding area within a few days.

stages.name: Ebb
stages.desc: Some Sea Terrors have behaviors that match the regularity of the tide's ebb and flow. Observant Hunters use this as an opportunity to go forth and eliminate them.

stages.name: Hunger and Thirst
stages.desc: When there is not enough food on land, head to the sea.

stages.name: Crime and Punishment
stages.desc: These rogues were unable to extract any wealth from the Sea Terrors, so they turned their blades on their fellow men. This time, they struck a sheet of metal.

stages.name: Troglodyte Tribe
stages.desc:
They are crueler, more intractable, and more belligerent than their Sargonian kin. So save your breath and pick up your weapons. 
This stage has  that greatly increases the ATK and ASPD of units that pass over it, but makes them lose HP over time.

stages.name: Craft Brewer Killer
stages.desc: Faced with a past-due shipment, a broken-down car, and now a swarm of Sea Terrors drunk off his slated-for-delivery craft brews, his insanity seems rather justifiable.

stages.name: Sanguinarch's Legacy
stages.desc: There may not be much of a difference between blood and seawater, at least in form. Therefore, anywhere the azure waves still ripple, the Sanguinarch's offspring can still survive.

stages.name: Precarious Defense
stages.desc: With the proper tactical decisions and everyone's mutual support, there may yet be a way to survive this savagery and madness.

stages.name: The Nest
stages.desc: The Sea Terror's nest extends in all directions, and intruders quickly find themselves trapped from two sides. By the time they manage to kill one that volunteered itself to stay behind as distraction, its kin have long since relocated.

stages.name: Vortex
stages.desc: The ocean seeks to devour all, and the cultists rouse the vortex. Sea Terrors gladly rush forth, preying on the food caught within.

stages.name: Looking Forward, Looking Back
stages.desc:
Enemies in front, enemies behind. Perhaps humans have fought long and hard, just to find a little piece of land free of Seaborn.
 Operators deployed on it and enemies who have passed it will take constant damage, but their ATK and ASPD will be increased significantly

stages.name: Underground Ascension
stages.desc: Some Seaborn have developed a rudimentary ability to fly. Though their progeny are unable to take to the blue skies, they have already leapt over chasms that humans cannot hope to cross in a lifetime.

stages.name: Blaze of Glory
stages.desc:
A mental roasting is sometimes more terrifying than getting burnt alive, and the leader of the Tara is an unquenchable fire that scorches one's soul. 
This stage has  that periodically inflict damage to units standing on it.

stages.name: Territorial Tendencies
stages.desc:
These irascible Originium slugs have lived here all their lives. To them, creatures from the outside fall into one of two camps: food, or intruders. 
This stage has  that periodically inflict damage to units standing on it.

stages.name: Seacavern Sandstorm
stages.desc:
Nature does not provide the conditions for sandstorms to form inside caves, but Originium Arts can accomplish that. 
This stage has  that halves the DEF of friendly units deployed on it.

stages.name: Nethersea Brand Land
stages.desc:
For the Sea Terrors, this is of course a paradise. Were it possible, they would want the nethersea brand to spread across all of Terra. 
This stage is filled with  that continuously seeks to expand, inflicting damage and Nervous Impairment. Try to counteract it by making use of  and .

stages.name: Hunting Grounds
stages.desc: Based on the situation, it was clear who the prey was. Based on the results, it was also very clear who the hunter was.

stages.name: The Revered Abyss
stages.desc:
If Seaborn are able to live in peace with the cultists, then it may indeed be possible for them to coexist with all of humanity. 
This stage has  that will deplete Life Point if destroyed, and  that launch enemy mercenary drones.

stages.name: Guns and Order
stages.desc:
New order is born when the old ways no longer work. Guns and etched ammo temporarily ensure the security of order.
There are <'Gelato Stops'> on the battlefield, which restore allied SP when captured.

stages.name: Mechanical Menace
stages.desc: In order to avoid being overrun by Sea Terrors, humanity has devised a set of mass extinction-level annihilation protocols for a swarm of unmanned drones. While this can indeed temporarily hold back the Sea Terrors, if it ever goes out of control...

stages.name: The Faithful's Utopia
stages.desc:
While the Sea Terrors prey upon humans on land, some humans worship and protect them in the ocean's depths. It certainly reflects one aspect of the human race—whether you choose to call it diversity or inferiority is up to you. 
This stage has  that launch enemy mercenary drones.

stages.name: Out of Control
stages.desc: The Church of the Deep cultists afflicted by late-stage Oripathy demonstrated clearly to we many the threat that Originium could pose. But, we many will eventually adapt. It's only a matter of time.

stages.name: The Piper's Call
stages.desc: The Seaborn do not need to evolve the ability to play and appreciate music, but certain individuals do wish to know why humans are so fascinated by it.

stages.name: Ubi bona somnia
stages.desc:
Do Seaborn dream? And what do they dream of? We don't have answers to those questions, but we do know that they don't like being disturbed when they're resting. 
This stage has  that can block the enemy's path.

stages.name: Pool of Procreation
stages.desc: Evolution is a gradual process. When a Seaborn continuously adapts and removes its own shortcomings, it will eventually become as perfect as a Leviathan.

stages.name: The Spread
stages.desc:
Survival is the Seaborn's raison d'etre; therefore, no matter how aggressive and expansionary they may seem in the eyes of other species, to them, these behaviors will always be nothing more than survival. 
This stage has  that continuously seeks to expand, inflicting damage and Nervous Impairment.

stages.name: Ember Phalanx
stages.desc: The march of the dead has reached the depths of the ocean. The ambiguity of their life or death has led the Seaborn to follow and observe them.

stages.name: Water and Fire Union
stages.desc:
Volcanoes erupt, the ocean trembles, and sleeping creatures stir. Between molten lava and icy waters, life will continue to persist. 
This stage has  that halves the DEF of friendly units deployed on it.

stages.name: Deep Cognition
stages.desc:
Some Seaborn individuals have mimicked the humans' opposing forces to simulate external conflicts. With their learning ability and cohesive efficiency, the humans' so-called combined arms and tactical strategies will soon become meaningless. 
This stage provides  that can be deployed to block the enemy's path.

stages.name: Requiem Aeternam
stages.desc:
Iberia is falling apart, and the Saint's life is coming to an end. He can no longer speak clearly, but as long as the aberrant sound within the sea still continues to ring, he will continue to fight. 
This stage has  that prevent your units inside from being targeted by enemy ranged attacks.

stages.name: Avoid Unsafe Roads
stages.desc: At the conclusion of the conspiracy and sin, a 'gravestone' awaits you. It is time to end it all. Friendly units have greatly reduced ATK and DEF; Natural DP regeneration is greatly reduced, and Redeployment Time is doubled.

stages.name: Eternal Wrath
stages.desc:
An old man has witnessed the second destruction of his homeland, the bitterness and wrath in his heart exterminating his rationality. Slaughter is the only language he has left to express himself. 
Only one round of Ammo is loaded. 
This stage has  that prevent your units inside from being targeted by enemy ranged attacks.

stages.name: Separation and Unity
stages.desc: After being transformed by cutting-edge Ægir technology, this Bishop of the Deep now has two bodies unified by a single will. He has created a completely new form of life.

stages.name: Two Forms, One Heart
stages.desc:
A Bishop of the Deep transformed a portion of his own flesh into a Seaborn connected with we many, allowing him to have a foot in both the human and Seaborn realms. 
The Seaborn body has reduced HP, but attacks with greater ferocity.

stages.name: Course Correction
stages.desc:
Regardless of whether or not it succeeds against your resistance, we many will learn their lesson and continue to move forward on their evolutionary path. 
This stage has  that can block the enemy's path.

stages.name: Route Revision
stages.desc:
The Seaborn are not prophets—their evolutionary path is full of mistakes and losses. So they've evolved individuals adept in trial and error to prevent the collective from making the same mistake twice. 
The number of hits required to spawn a Fractal is reduced. 
This stage has  that can block the enemy's path.

stages.name: Curse of Cognition
stages.desc: All things in existence are paradoxical, and those who wander within the boundaries of cognition are doomed to break apart. Until she reaffirms the meaning of her own existence, she will forever be a slave to her id.

stages.name: 'Fate's Favored Child'
stages.desc:
Precisely because he is always fighting, fate continues to favor him, constantly throwing him into misery and calamity, and eagerly awaiting his undoing. 
This stage has  that can block the enemy's path.

stages.name: Destiny of We Many
stages.desc: The fate of humankind, the Seaborn, Terra, and what remains of Skadi's humanity shall all be realized in this final moment.

stages.name: Glory of Humanity
stages.desc: The moment Izumik recalls his offspring and performs the genetic modification of the swarm's astralization, Mizuki will demonstrate the potential and spirituality of humanity for himself and the entire Seaborn swarm.

stages.name: Hunting Group
stages.desc: You’ve met a group of wasteland dwellers on a hunt. The group’s leader has targeted you with his crossbow—evidently, he doesn’t particularly care what his prey is.

stages.name: Chilling Curse
stages.desc: Cruel frost, an innocent ballad, and the curse of the endless tundras coalesce into a Frozen Catastrophe that devours everything. This stage has  that periodically apply the Cold status in an area around them.

stages.name: Reality
stages.desc: Everyone has their own perspectives and a hill they're willing to die on. Now that a decision has been made, the consequences must be seen through to the end.

stages.name: Fanatical Flames
stages.desc: The flames seethe and burn, like their untethered sanity, like the rage within your heart.

stages.name: 'Joy' From a Box
stages.desc: Oh, a Sea Terror Doblón. Not bad, but is there anything more interesting in the chest? Like, a sculpture that spits out Doblones, or... a pure gold Sea Terror or something?

stages.name: Fincatching in a Dry Pond
stages.desc: As the old saying goes: 'Always spare some dignity around others, for you never know who you might meet again later.' But then you tell yourself—is there really any need to hold water for Cannot? After victory, you may loot Cannot's shop

stages.name: See the Big Picture
stages.desc: The journey is about to come to an end, and the denouement is about to be written. At a time like this, squeezing every drop of value from the merchant's wares is not out of the question. After victory, you may loot Cannot's shop.

stages.name: Indecisiveness
stages.desc: He lacks any sense of initiative. If faced with a fork in the road, he'll spend half the day just thinking about which path to take.

stages.name: Supervision Site
stages.desc:
Even after clocking out, these employees won't forget to set aside time to help the boss take care of some problems. As a reward, Duck Lord might even be willing to let them use the restroom during working hours? 
This stage has  that can block the enemy's path.

stages.name: Right in the Kisser
stages.desc:
No matter how painful the lancers' thrusts or the broodmother's baleful spawns might be, is there anything that hurts as badly as Gopnik's punch? 
This stage has  that greatly increases the ATK and ASPD of units that pass over it, but makes them lose HP over time.

stages.name: Tail Between Legs
stages.desc: As annoying as this crybaby is, you have no choice but to admit that he's really good at making a getaway. This stage has a  that can disable the Invisibility effect of nearby enemies.

stages.name: A New Day, A New Duck
stages.desc: Since when did 'a little friction' between the two of you become such a commonplace occurrence?

enemy_1159_swfmob.name: Delinquent
enemy_1159_swfmob.desc: The most commonly seen get-up of Dossoles's delinquents. They intermingle with tourists, ready to join in a fight at any time. Agile and nimble, they can dodge ranged Physical attacks.
enemy_1159_swfmob.ability: Has a chance to dodge Physical attacks when not blocked

enemy_1158_divman.name: Diver
enemy_1158_divman.desc: Their daily tasks involve cleaning up trash from the sea. The barrier to entry is low but the pay is surprisingly good, so there are many who sign up for it.
enemy_1158_divman.ability: Immune to Erosion. While in water, gains increased ATK and Invisibility

enemy_1007_slime.name: Originium Slug
enemy_1007_slime.desc: An infected wild creature. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.

enemy_1007_slime_2.name: Originium Slug α
enemy_1007_slime_2.desc: An infected wild creature that is more dangerous than a normal Originium Slug. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.

enemy_2001_duckmi.name: Duck Lord
enemy_2001_duckmi.desc: A famous Columbian investor, entrepreneur, and master of 'Duck Power.' Out of the public eye, enjoys kicking back with others...
enemy_2001_duckmi.ability:
Cannot be blocked.
Gains significantly increased Movement Speed after being attacked

enemy_2002_bearmi.name: Gopnik
enemy_2002_bearmi.desc: The Ursus security guard hired by Duck Lord. Powerful, but not very smart. Mainly responsible for carrying luggage for Duck Lord.

enemy_2034_sythef.name: Crying Thief
enemy_2034_sythef.desc: A Perro who left his family to seek out a new life... but all that people know is that he is always weeping.

enemy_2035_sybox.name: Chest Seaborn
enemy_2035_sybox.desc: Sea Terror disguised as a treasure chest, often used by Bishops of the Deep to protect hideouts.

enemy_2085_skzjxd.name: Fatty
enemy_2085_skzjxd.desc: Duck Lord's associate and apprentice, an opportunist with a slick tongue. Frequently appears on dubious TV shows, preaching about how he cares little for losses or gains, as they are part of the natural money-making process.
enemy_2085_skzjxd.ability:
Always faces the direction with the most Operators.
Takes 80% less Physical/Arts damage from the front.

enemy_1100_scorpn.name: Training Gloompincer
enemy_1100_scorpn.desc: Infected creatures commonly found in the great lakes and rivers of Kazimierz, captured and raised by casters and used as tools to train knights and entertain fight spectators.

enemy_1021_bslime.name: Infused Originium Slug
enemy_1021_bslime.desc: An infected wild creature that has become unstable due to assimilating a large amount of Originium into its abdomen, exploding upon death. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.
enemy_1021_bslime.ability: Death deals a large amount of Physical damage to surrounding units

enemy_1004_mslime.name: Acid Originium Slug
enemy_1004_mslime.desc: An infected wild creature that can secrete corrosive fluids strong enough to dissolve armor. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.
enemy_1004_mslime.ability: Every attack reduces the enemy's DEF

enemy_1016_diaman.name: Metal Crab
enemy_1016_diaman.desc: An infected wild creature, completely covered in metal. Though most conventional weapons are not very effective against them, they aren't as heavy as they seem, and also move slowly. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.

enemy_1047_sagent.name: Sniper
enemy_1047_sagent.desc: A basic ranged soldier. They have very long range. Their sniper rifles can launch special ammunition capable of stunning our Operators.
enemy_1047_sagent.ability: After two attacks, the next attack Stuns its target

enemy_1019_jshoot.name: Invisible Crossbowman
enemy_1019_jshoot.desc: One of Reunion's ranged soldiers. The ghillie suit they wear makes it almost impossible to detect them before they engage in combat. Therefore, teamwork is needed to defeat them.

enemy_2021_syfish.name: Bone Sea Drifter
enemy_2021_syfish.desc: Sea Terrors travel in large swarms, seeking carcasses to feast on and becoming food for other Sea Terrors themselves.

enemy_1095_ccripr_2.name: Tiacauh Ripper
enemy_1095_ccripr_2.desc: An extremely fervent Tiacauh warrior with very fast attack speed. Each strike now deals noteworthy damage, and those caught off guard will be quickly subdued by a rain of blows.
enemy_1095_ccripr_2.ability: Every attack reduces the enemy's DEF

enemy_1010_demon.name: Sarkaz Greatswordsman
enemy_1010_demon.desc: A mercenary from Sarkaz. The Originium inside them has boosted their physical abilities, allowing them to wield larger weapons with ease and withstand more damage.

enemy_1147_dshond.name: Shell Sea Runner
enemy_1147_dshond.desc: A Sea Terror that grew four limbs, but lacks an internal skeleton. It seems to have once hunted quadrupeds on land.

enemy_1148_dssbr_2.name: Nourished Slider
enemy_1148_dssbr_2.desc: A Sea Terror that crawled onto the shore and has sated its hunger. Its appendages can cause protein denaturation.

enemy_1171_durokt.name: Dublinn Flying Soldier
enemy_1171_durokt.desc: A flying soldier in the Dublinn forces. The steam propulsion device they use originates from a secret laboratory owned by the Victorian military. As it has only recently been deployed in battle, its power output is still unstable.

enemy_2022_syzeni.name: Ocean Stonecutter
enemy_2022_syzeni.desc: Sea Terror with a hard shell, protecting the swarm from enemies.
enemy_2022_syzeni.ability: When your units enter its attack range for the first time, temporarily stops moving and gains increased DEF.

enemy_1007_slime_3.name: Originium Slug β
enemy_1007_slime_3.desc: An infected wild creature that is more dangerous than an Originium Slug α. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.

enemy_1080_sotidp.name: Guerrilla Herald
enemy_1080_sotidp.desc: Patriot's guerilla fighters employ a special language to communicate and synergize their coordinated operations on the battlefield. The heralds are important links within their communication system. While present, they will issue tactical commands to increase the offensive and defensive abilities of nearby allies.
enemy_1080_sotidp.ability: Increases the ATK and DEF of enemy units when on the battlefield.

enemy_1083_sotiab.name: Guerrilla Siegebreaker
enemy_1083_sotiab.desc: One of Patriot's elite assault fighters. These troops specialize in assaults and airborne operations into enemy backlines. They wear medium armor and boast very strong individual combat capabilities. After receiving tactical commands, they will gain greatly increased combat capabilities and have greatly increased attack power.
enemy_1083_sotiab.ability:
Able to land directly onto the battlefield.
When Inspired, gains greatly increased ATK.

enemy_1187_krghd.name: Clawbeast
enemy_1187_krghd.desc: A wild beast that began appearing in the wilderness several decades ago. Depending on differences in the environment, their bodies and organs undergo various changes.

enemy_1068_snmage.name: Yeti Caster
enemy_1068_snmage.desc: A basic caster of the Yeti Squadron hailing from teams of infected guerilla warriors in the Northwestern Tundra. Through special Arts and devices, they can inflict Cold on our Operators, reducing their attack speed and making them vulnerable to being Frozen.
enemy_1068_snmage.ability: After two attacks, the next attack applies the Cold effect

enemy_1067_snslime.name: Infused Glacial Originium Slug
enemy_1067_snslime.desc: An infected wild creature native to colder environments that has become unstable due to assimilating a large amount of Originium into its abdomen. Upon death, they will explode and freeze nearby Operators. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by Yeti Squadron members for various tactical purposes.
enemy_1067_snslime.ability: Upon death, deals Physical damage in an area and applies Cold to affected targets

enemy_1190_krgbow.name: Icefield Hunter
enemy_1190_krgbow.desc: A hunter living on the icefields. Creatures on the icefields often form groups, and the hunters have become skilled at finding the weakest link in a group of prey.
enemy_1190_krgbow.ability: Prioritizes attacking the target with the lowest DEF

enemy_1064_snsbr.name: Yeti Operative
enemy_1064_snsbr.desc: A basic melee combatants of the Yeti Squadron hailing from teams of infected guerilla warriors in the Northwestern Tundra. They are skilled at attacking frozen Operators.
enemy_1064_snsbr.ability: Attack power increases when attacking Frozen targets

enemy_1027_mob.name: Rioter
enemy_1027_mob.desc: An enemy combatant of unknown identity. They are often found wherever there is turmoil and unrest. They wear a face mask to conceal their identity and use haphazardly-made weapons to engage in melee combat.

enemy_1132_sarchn.name: Mutant Rock Spider
enemy_1132_sarchn.desc: A rock spider infected by an Originiutant. Due to their similar appearances, rock spiders are often mistakenly believed to be a close relative of Infused Originium Slugs. However, the latest research shows that they are two completely different species.

enemy_1130_infstr.name: Originiutant Puppet
enemy_1130_infstr.desc: A corpse being controlled by a parasitic Originiutant Excrescence.

enemy_1137_plasm.name: Originiutant Excrescence
enemy_1137_plasm.desc: A twisted organism born under the combined effects of radiation mutation and Originium infection.
enemy_1137_plasm.ability: Drains 1 Life Point when entering the Objective Point

enemy_1049_eagent.name: Agent
enemy_1049_eagent.desc: A caster who utilizes coordinated tactics. They are able to enhance their melee attacks with Arts damage, and employ a variety of techniques to break through unprepared defenders.
enemy_1049_eagent.ability: Attacks temporarily reduce allied units' Block

enemy_2023_sypult.name: Retching Broodmother
enemy_2023_sypult.desc: Sea Terror mothers that throw fake offspring at oncoming threats, allowing the true offspring to flee.
enemy_2023_sypult.ability:
Can only attack ground targets
Summons 1  near the target when dealing damage

enemy_2024_synut.name: Baleful Broodling
enemy_2024_synut.desc: An extension of the broodmother's body, identical to newly-born offspring.
enemy_2024_synut.ability:
Unable to move
Gradually loses HP over time
Attacks inflict additional Corrosion.

enemy_1009_lurker.name: Lurker
enemy_1009_lurker.desc: One of Reunion's special soldiers. They move swiftly and stealthily, making it almost impossible to detect them before they engage in combat. Therefore, teamwork is needed in order to defeat them.

enemy_1002_nsabr.name: Soldier
enemy_1002_nsabr.desc: One of Reunion's basic melee soldiers. Equipped with illegally obtained weapons and armor, they wear masks to conceal their identities during operations.

enemy_1006_shield_2.name: Heavy Defender Leader
enemy_1006_shield_2.desc: One of Reunion's melee soldiers, more dangerous than a normal Heavy Defender. Equipped with a full set of heavy armor, they have ample combat experience and are extremely hard to defeat. As they may have had formal training, proceed with caution.

enemy_1035_haxe.name: Armed Militant
enemy_1035_haxe.desc: An enemy of unknown identity. Equipped with a complex array of armor, a gas mask, and tanks of unidentifiable substances, they attack using giant blunt instruments. Due to their logic-defying equipment, they are simply called 'Armed Militants.' Though their movement is slow, even a single swing of their weapon is sure to inflict massive damage. Proceed with caution.

enemy_1267_nhpbr.name: Sarkaz Witherer
enemy_1267_nhpbr.desc: Part of the Nachzehrer army. Death cannot stop their corrupting presence, as their corpses continue to corrupt the land.
enemy_1267_nhpbr.ability: Releases Corruption when defeated, causing your nearby units to continuously lose HP

enemy_1269_nhfly.name: Seed of Withering
enemy_1269_nhfly.desc: Originium Arts creation of the Nachzehrer King. Mindless except for an obsession with self-destruction. They create death for others to feed upon.
enemy_1269_nhfly.ability:
Flying unit.
Self-destructs when near an Operator, dealing Physical damage

enemy_1271_nhkodo.name: Sarkaz Wither Devourer
enemy_1271_nhkodo.desc: Part of the Nachzehrer army. Devours any obstacle in its way, including the living. Anything and everything is food to it.
enemy_1271_nhkodo.ability:
When blocked for the first time, devours the blocker. DEF is greatly increased, but Movement Speed is reduced
Returns devoured Operator when defeated

enemy_1270_nhstlk.name: Rotchaser
enemy_1270_nhstlk.desc: Originium Arts creation of the Nachzehrer King. Fast-moving, they do not understand the concept of healing. They exist only to create death, and the power of healing robs them of their powers.
enemy_1270_nhstlk.ability: Attacks cause the target to take Arts damage over time; effect removed when the target is healed

enemy_1078_sotisc.name: Guerrilla Fighter
enemy_1078_sotisc.desc: A basic guerilla fighter. After receiving tactical commands, they will gain increased capabilities and utilize a full range of offensive maneuvers.

enemy_1079_sotisp.name: Guerrilla Sniper
enemy_1079_sotisp.desc: A basic guerilla sniper who has received professional, military-grade sharpshooting skills. After receiving tactical commands, they will gain increased capabilities and attack two targets at once.
enemy_1079_sotisp.ability: When Inspired, attacks one additional target.

enemy_1083_sotiab_2.name: Guerrilla Siegebreaker Leader
enemy_1083_sotiab_2.desc: The cream of the crop among Patriot's elite assault fighters. Though boasting greater individual combat capabilities than normal Siegebreakers, they often end up behind bars. After receiving tactical commands, they will gain greatly increased combat capabilities and have greatly increased attack power.
enemy_1083_sotiab_2.ability:
Able to land directly onto the battlefield.
When Inspired, gains massively increased ATK.

enemy_1013_airdrp_2.name: Airborne Soldier Leader
enemy_1013_airdrp_2.desc: One of Reunion's special melee soldiers, more dangerous than a normal Airborne Soldier. They are equipped with modified disposable jetpacks made from looted parts. Despite their fairly rudimentary equipment, Reunion uses them to great effect when attacking insufficiently protected areas.
enemy_1013_airdrp_2.ability: Able to land directly onto the battlefield.

enemy_1082_soticn.name: Guerrilla Mortar
enemy_1082_soticn.desc: One of Patriot's elite suppression troops. Adept at long-ranged shelling, these fighters move more slowly because of their medium armor but also enjoy increased protection. After receiving tactical commands, they will gain greatly increased combat capabilities and have significantly increased attack speed.
enemy_1082_soticn.ability: When Inspired, gains greatly increased ASPD.

enemy_1092_mdgint.name: Mudrock Colossus
enemy_1092_mdgint.desc: This Originium construct, crafted by Mudrock, does not possess sentience and must be directly controlled by Mudrock. Though bulky and slow, they are extremely durable and boast terrifying physical damage and survivability.

enemy_1096_ccwitch.name: Tiacauh Ritualist
enemy_1096_ccwitch.desc: The very few Originium Arts users in Acahualla will often serve as doctors for their tribe. Despite not developing their Originium Arts, their medical experience lets them strike where it hurts.
enemy_1096_ccwitch.ability: Gains DEF when blocked, and deals Physical damage with melee attacks.

enemy_1098_cchmpn_2.name: Tiacauh Champion
enemy_1098_cchmpn_2.desc: An incredibly formidable Tiacauh warrior, with the qualifications to become a Warchief. These warriors harbor great pride in their combat abilities, and live for the thrill of the duel no matter where they are. Their single-mindedness allows them to shrug off all outside interference.
enemy_1098_cchmpn_2.ability: When blocked, this unit will only receive damage from the unit blocking it, and the DEF of both units is decreased.

enemy_1093_ccsbr_2.name: Tiacauh Guard
enemy_1093_ccsbr_2.desc: A braver, fiercer warrior who uses a crude spear to attack. Years of training have given these warriors tough physiques, and they fear not even the fiercest attacks.

enemy_1150_dsjely.name: Floating Sea Drifter
enemy_1150_dsjely.desc: A Sea Terror that floats in the air. It seems that the next generation will be even more proficient at swimming through the air.
enemy_1150_dsjely.ability:
Flying unit.
Attacks inflict additional Nervous Impairment

enemy_1152_dsurch.name: Pocket Sea Crawler
enemy_1152_dsurch.desc: A Sea Terror imbued with impurities. The density of its body tissue surpasses that of some alloys. Victims stabbed by it will feel as if they have been set alight.
enemy_1152_dsurch.ability: Whenever a certain percentage of HP is lost, immediately deal a large amount of Arts damage and Nervous Impairment to nearby units

enemy_1001_bigbo_2.name: Big Adam
enemy_1001_bigbo_2.desc: One of Reunion's squad leaders. He wears a heavy blast suit and attacks with an electric chainsaw. Bears a remarkable similarity to Big Bob, but does not appear to be the same person. It is unknown whether there is any relationship between the two of them. Data suggests that he may be even more dangerous than Big Bob. Due to his devastating attacks and extreme survivability, proceed with utmost caution.

enemy_1148_dssbr.name: Deep Sea Slider
enemy_1148_dssbr.desc: A Sea Terror that crawled onto the shore. It brandishes its tough appendages menacingly.

enemy_1220_dzoms.name: Touch of the Sanguinarch
enemy_1220_dzoms.desc: A creation of the Sanguinarch of Vampires' Originium Arts, flexible and difficult to defeat. According to rumors, the Sanguinarch is able to snatch the blood of other creatures, imbuing new life into it and taking it under his control.
enemy_1220_dzoms.ability:
Reborn Creation (takes greatly reduced Physical and Arts damage)
Units blocking it gain +1 Block (up to a maximum of +6)

enemy_1222_dpvt.name: Sarkaz Heirbearer Warrior
enemy_1222_dpvt.desc: A warrior of the Sarkaz Royal Court blessed with the living blood of the Sanguinarch of Vampires. After falling in battle for the first time, his blood will continue to advance in his place.
enemy_1222_dpvt.ability: Will be reborn as a  after falling in battle for the first time

enemy_1224_dsuply_2.name: Sarkaz Heirbearer Retrofit ASV
enemy_1224_dsuply_2.desc: An armored supply vehicle meticulously blessed by the Sanguinarch of Vampires. While on the battlefield, enhances Gift of the Sanguinarch and Touch of the Sanguinarch.
enemy_1224_dsuply_2.ability: While in battle, Reborn Creations have increased ATK, and their attacks will additionally deal Necrosis

enemy_1221_dzomg.name: Gift of the Sanguinarch
enemy_1221_dzomg.desc: A creation of the Sanguinarch of Vampires' Originium Arts that bears greater vitality. One who was powerful in life will continue to be a great threat to their enemies even after being reshaped by the power of the Sanguinarch's blessing.
enemy_1221_dzomg.ability:
Reborn Creation (takes greatly reduced Physical and Arts damage)
Units blocking it gain +1 Block (up to a maximum of +6)

enemy_1221_dzomg_2.name: Gift of Magnanimity
enemy_1221_dzomg_2.desc: A creation of the Sanguinarch of Vampires' Originium Arts that bears exceptional vitality. One who was powerful in life will continue to be a great threat to their enemies even after being reshaped by the power of the Sanguinarch's blessing. Therefore, those who continue ought to thank the Sanguinarch for his magnanimity.
enemy_1221_dzomg_2.ability:
Reborn Creation (takes greatly reduced Physical and Arts damage)
Units blocking it gain +1 Block (up to a maximum of +6)

enemy_1062_rager.name: Enraged Possessed Soldier
enemy_1062_rager.desc: An enemy combatant who has slowly descended into primal madness as their lifeforce is consumed. The infection has penetrated their bone marrow, and they are unusually aggressive as a result. The appearance of such an aberration on the battlefield is almost beyond imagination.
enemy_1062_rager.ability: Will gradually lose HP over time

enemy_1063_rageth.name: Enraged Possessed Thrower
enemy_1063_rageth.desc: An enemy ranged combatant who has slowly descended into primal madness as their lifeforce is consumed. The infection has penetrated their bone marrow, and they will hurl whatever they can get their hands on at their targets. The appearance of such an aberration on the battlefield is almost beyond imagination.
enemy_1063_rageth.ability: Will gradually lose HP over time

enemy_1021_bslime_2.name: Infused Originium Slug α
enemy_1021_bslime_2.desc: An infected wild creature that has become unstable due to assimilating a large amount of Originium into its abdomen, exploding upon death. More dangerous than a normal Infused Originium Slug. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.
enemy_1021_bslime_2.ability: Death deals a large amount of Physical damage to surrounding units

enemy_1000_gopro_3.name: Rabid Hound Pro
enemy_1000_gopro_3.desc: A creature controlled by a Reunion recon squad that is more dangerous than a Hound Pro. Monitoring equipment is strapped to its body. Due to their speed and evasiveness, they can easily slip through enemy defenses and cause heavy damage.

enemy_1008_ghost.name: Wraith
enemy_1008_ghost.desc: One of Reunion's engineers. Though unarmed, they utilize their agility and cunning to slip through defenses in order to sabotage mechanical and electrical equipment.

enemy_1026_aghost.name: Wraith Leader
enemy_1026_aghost.desc: One of Reunion's engineers. Due to being better trained and more heavily equipped, they are harder to neutralize in time, even if they have been detected. They utilize their agility and cunning to slip through defenses in order to sabotage mechanical and electrical equipment.

enemy_1076_bsthmr.name: Demolitionist
enemy_1076_bsthmr.desc: A high-level elite Reunion combatant who wears heavy-duty, explosion-proof protective gear. Their special Originium-powered hammers have massive destructive range, and must be dealt with cautiously.
enemy_1076_bsthmr.ability: Attacks cause an explosion, dealing Splash damage to units around the primary target.

enemy_1161_tidmag.name: Tidal Caster
enemy_1161_tidmag.desc: Professionally trained casters whose duties involve simulating the tides of Dossoles's sea. Adept at using Originium Arts that mimic the erosive effect of the tides.
enemy_1161_tidmag.ability: Attacks deal damage to the target and all allied units in the target's four adjacent tiles, additionally inflicting Corrosion

enemy_1162_magmot.name: Caster Speedboat
enemy_1162_magmot.desc: A Tidal Caster on a speedboat. The Caster will continue battle after the vehicle is destroyed.
enemy_1162_magmot.ability:
Attacks deal damage to the target and all allied units in the target's four adjacent tiles, additionally inflicting Corrosion
The  will continue battle after the vehicle is destroyed

enemy_1113_empace.name: Imperial Striker
enemy_1113_empace.desc: The powerful infantry of the Ursus Army, the backbone of the Imperial Army's aggressive advance. They are versatile in offense and defense, melee and ranged. After sustain a certain amount of damage, their morale will peak, causing them to inflict more destruction.
enemy_1113_empace.ability: Gains significantly increased ATK when HP is under 50%

enemy_1038_lunmag_2.name: Spec Ops Caster Leader
enemy_1038_lunmag_2.desc: One of Reunion's elite Casters. Their armor makes them more resistant to physical damage compared to regular Casters. They have mastered Arts that allows them to attack two targets at once. Proceed with caution.

enemy_1113_empace_2.name: Elite Imperial Striker
enemy_1113_empace_2.desc: The powerful, battle-tested infantry of the Ursus Army, the backbone of the Imperial Army's aggressive advance. They are versatile in offense and defense, melee and ranged. After sustain a certain amount of damage, their morale will peak, causing them to inflict more destruction. Along with the Shieldguards, they formed the front line of the Ursus Army.
enemy_1113_empace_2.ability: Gains significantly increased ATK when HP is under 50%

enemy_1038_lunmag.name: Spec Ops Caster
enemy_1038_lunmag.desc: One of Reunion's Casters. Their armor makes them more resistant to physical damage compared to regular Casters.

enemy_2025_syufo.name: Skimming Sea Drifter
enemy_2025_syufo.desc: Some Sea Terrors have begun to adapt to the sky, able to glide above the waves for extended periods. Perhaps this branch will eventually evolve to make their home in the heavens.
enemy_2025_syufo.ability:
Low-Altitude Hovering
Attacks inflict additional Corrosion.

enemy_1166_dusbr_2.name: Dublinn Scout Leader

relics.usage: +10 Light

relics.name: Flamecaller Originium Slug
relics.desc: A species of wild Originium slug, with plumes of dim flame periodically bursting from its back. Through modern scientific research, it was determined that the creature's incendiary phenomenon was the result of a chemical reaction, not the resurrection of their kin's souls.
relics.usage: +15 Light

relics.name: Pathfinder Fin
relics.desc: An asexual marine creature endemic to the coasts of Iberia, named because they are often said to guide fincatchers back to shore. These days, they are rarely seen, and are now regarded as a symbol of luck by the new generation of Iberians.
relics.usage: +30 Light

relics.name: Blunt Claws - Training
relics.desc: A formidable set of equipment to provide strength, an indomitable heart to press forward bravely.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Vanguard Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Bend Spears - Training
relics.desc: Some people use swords because they don't know how to use crossbows or Arts. Others use swords because that's all they need.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Guard Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Iron Guard - Training
relics.desc: Scars symbolize glory, but who will remember the soldiers who were cut a bit too deeply?
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Defender Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Fatal Bolts - Training
relics.desc: 'Either you learn to control your ignition, or you learn to discern your target. Got it?'
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Sniper Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Broken Wand - Training
relics.desc: 'After watching the 'performances' of certain casters, I decided to quietly put away the Leithanian textbook.'
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Caster Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Stalwart Aid - Training
relics.desc: Their tactics are always diverse and colorful.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Supporter Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Healer's Path - Training
relics.desc: When the day comes that everyone is a doctor capable of curing the disease known as life, we will no longer need doctors.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Medic Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Rusted Blade - Training
relics.desc: If you haven't properly prepared for a live combat drill with a Specialist Operator, you should be prepared for a thorough beating.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Specialist Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Fissured Restraints
relics.desc: It seems this sturdy band was once used to restrain something terrifying... Help...
relics.usage: All enemy units have -7% ATK

relics.name: 'Silent Squad'
relics.desc: A set of exploration drones remotely controlled by the Rhodes Island Engineering Department. More efficient and safer than ever, they provide operators on field missions the omnidirectional support and defense they need.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +35% ATK, +35% DEF, and +45% HP

relics.name: Abyssal Wyrdmask
relics.desc: An eldritch mask not belonging to any culture you’re familiar with, capable of making people lose their will to fight... and unconsciously fall into a state of deep contemplation.
relics.usage: All enemy units have -12% ATK

relics.name: Godmother's Token
relics.desc: A laurel of bones, the token of a Sicilian noblewoman. Its orderly appearance symbolizes the desire to stabilize turmoil. Genuine or counterfeit, it still demands you to kneel.
relics.usage: All enemy units have -17% ATK

relics.name: Worn-out Group Photo
relics.desc: Do you know someone in the photo?
relics.usage: All enemy units have -12% DEF

relics.name: Sea Terror Jerky
relics.desc: Once their desire to survive overwhelms their terror, the famished Iberians will devour this 'food,' subsequently turning into Sea Terrors on some ordinary day.
relics.usage: All enemy units have -21% DEF

relics.name: Rosmontis's Embrace
relics.desc: 'Who will be the judge?'
relics.usage: All enemy units have -30% DEF

relics.name: Gold-Plated Die
relics.desc: The gold-plated die has one side representing life. As for the other nineteen sides...
relics.usage: All enemy units have -15% HP

relics.name: 'The Whisperer in Darknight'
relics.desc: A black vinyl. DO. NOT. TOUCH. IT.
relics.usage: All enemy units have -10% HP

relics.name: 'Profound Silence'
relics.desc: A painting that depicts the greatest disaster in Iberian history. Its brushstrokes, colors, and images are all meaningless. 'The Ægir know. The Ægir know. The Ægir know.'
relics.usage: All enemy units have -20% HP

relics.name: Oriron Round Shield
relics.desc: Before the use of Oriron became widespread in today's industrial sector, the material was frequently used to forge military weapons like this one.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +15% DEF

relics.name: Military Mirror Armor
relics.desc: This mirror armor used to be able to withstand most attacks on the chest, but with the fall of Gaul, such archaic forms of armor have become museum pieces.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +25% DEF

relics.name: Old Steam Armor
relics.desc: Even this old, outdated steam armor seems to carry with it the resplendence of the era when the Victorian monarchs cast their glory across half the world.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +35% DEF

relics.name: Emperor's Favor
relics.desc: A very sharp letter opener. It was one of the favorite possessions of the last Ursus emperor, and as such it was very rarely used to actually open letters.
relics.usage: All friendly melee units have +15% ATK

relics.name: Royal Rapier
relics.desc: A highly decorative rapier for nobles. Used by Gaulish nobles first and foremost, though perhaps a little fragile for a military weapon.
relics.usage: All friendly melee units have +25% ATK

relics.name: Vieux Vanguard's Blade
relics.desc: Prior to its downfall, Gaul first witnessed the sacrifices of its Vieux Vanguards.
relics.usage: All friendly melee units have +35% ATK

relics.name: Necklace of the Presence
relics.desc: Faith hangs ten centimeters above your heart, spoken words flow into your blood, and bullets slide into your magazine.
relics.usage: All friendly ranged units have +15% ATK

relics.name: Silver Forks
relics.desc: One for dealing with actors who botch their performances, one for dealing with incompetent playwrights, and one for anyone who needs it.
relics.usage: All friendly ranged units have +25% ATK

relics.name: Damaged Revolver Cylinder
relics.desc: She never capitulated, even after enduring countless trials and tribulations. Her muzzle belched tongues of flame that burned like an angry sun, and the glow above her head was brighter than the daylight itself. Buried here is Outcast, our friend.
relics.usage: All friendly ranged units have +35% ATK

relics.name: Noxious Hemostatic Agent
relics.desc: 'After fermentation, the body fluids of this Originium slug can be used to stop bleeding.' —'Rhodes Island Wilderness Survival Guide'
relics.usage: All friendly units have +20% HP

relics.name: First Aid Kit
relics.desc: '...Use antibiotics and ethanol additives under professional supervision...' —Rim Billiton Production Safety Manual
relics.usage: All friendly units have +35% HP

relics.name: Unknown Instrument
relics.desc: Don't ask. It's for your own good.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +50% HP

relics.name: Rusted Razor
relics.desc: If you cut yourself with this, you know what will happen.
relics.usage: All enemies take +15% Physical damage

relics.name: Carriage Driver's Whip
relics.desc: As the troupe's carriage driver, he has no name and no past. His life is meaningful only when he whips his burdenbeasts.
relics.usage: All enemies take +25% Physical damage

relics.name: 'Avenger'
relics.desc: A gift from Theresis and set up by Theresa, this is the very first weapon that Ascalon received after her inaugural mission as part of the Military Council.
relics.usage: All enemies take +35% Physical damage

relics.name: Standard Anti-Riot Instrument
relics.desc: Standard equipment of the Ursus Guard. Comes with an intermittent blinding Originium Arts effect. Unfortunately, not many Guards out there remember this when push comes to shove.
relics.usage: All enemies take +20% Arts damage

relics.name: Emperor's Collection
relics.desc: The Sami revere their wild lands more than anything, and this fragment of the vast wilderness sleeps in the personal collection of the Emperor of Ursus.
relics.usage: All enemies take +30% Arts damage

relics.name: 'Brilliant Lament'
relics.desc: Their blood yet flows. He should not have left this place.
relics.usage: All enemies take +40% Arts damage

relics.name: Live Rose
relics.desc: Is it you who nourish her, or is it her who protects you?
relics.usage: All friendly units receive +20% healing effectiveness

relics.name: White Flower Crown
relics.desc: A floral veil used in Vampire rituals. Its once vibrant redness has been sucked empty, leaving nothing only a pale white color.
relics.usage: All friendly units receive +30% healing effectiveness

relics.name: Surging Feast
relics.desc: We've known how to indulge since birth. Do not resist your desires. Submit to them instead.
relics.usage: All friendly units receive +30% healing effectiveness and take -30% Elemental Injury

relics.name: Actor's Perfume
relics.desc: Troupe actors put this on before going onstage. Stable yet aromatic fragrances like this can help them ease pressure. That's how it seems on the surface, at least.
relics.usage: All friendly units recover 1% of their Max HP per second

relics.name: Designer's Ruler
relics.desc: Carefully measured with data and experience, those who wear this armor are known to be several magnitudes luckier.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +15% Physical Dodge

relics.name: 'Arts Killer'
relics.desc: Considering how Originium Arts and music go hand in hand in Leithanien, it's quite amazing someone came up with the bright idea of combating Arts with noise like this.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +15% Arts Dodge

relics.name: Dancer's Bracelets
relics.desc: The audience breaks into applause as the well-trained dancers waltz around the traps on stage. None of them are aware of the brushes with death the performers have just been through.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +10% Physical and Arts Dodge

relics.name: Doll House
relics.desc: All kids need at least one set.
relics.usage: +10 Starting DP

relics.name: Miniature Stage Model
relics.desc: The red paint has not dried yet. Careful. Don't get that paint on your hand.
relics.usage: +20 Starting DP

relics.name: Dreambind Castle Model
relics.desc: You've seen this place, and you once wandered the castle, yet you now hold it in your hand. Is this real, or is it all a dream?
relics.usage: +30 Starting DP

relics.name: Vortex Confluence
relics.desc: Rule the waves, rule the currents. Once, Ægir and the ocean were synonymous.
relics.usage: Specialist Operators have +30% ATK, and Caster Operators have +3% ATK

relics.name: Ocean Voyage
relics.desc: Twice they escaped to land, but in this they nevertheless still take pride: Ægir once measured the ocean.
relics.usage: Caster Operators have +30% ATK, and Specialist Operators have +3% ATK

relics.name: Jet-Black Dance Shoes
relics.desc: The Columbian dancer's steps are firm and strong, her taps reverberating loudly in the air as she strikes the ground.
relics.usage: Can deploy Melee Operators on High Ground tiles

relics.name: Pure White Dance Shoes
relics.desc: The Ursus dancer's steps are swift and elegant, her body moving as she wills.
relics.usage: Can deploy Ranged Operators on Low Ground tiles

relics.name: Viviparous Lily
relics.desc: An organism that defies common sense. A plant-like ability to reproduce, an animal-like desire to.
relics.usage: When a battle begins, reduces the DP Cost of all Operators by 3, then scrambles their DP Cost

relics.name: Chitinous Ripper
relics.desc: A more efficient way of growing an exoskeleton. It can tear prey apart, resist impact, and also be turned into a delicacy for its kin.
relics.usage: When a battle begins, a random Operator will have +100% ATK and +100% HP for that battle

relics.name: Ancient Gaulish Silver Coin
relics.desc: A coin once circulated in the now fallen nation 'Gaul.' It is in very good shape, making it highly valuable as a collector's item.
relics.usage: All Operators start with +6 SP

relics.name: Bank of Gaul Check
relics.desc: An extravagant checkbook plated in gold and silver. Can no longer be cashed.
relics.usage: All Operators start with +12 SP

relics.name: 'Second Economic Reform Act'
relics.desc: With this bill as the starting point, Gaul formally began its path to become a hegemonic empire.
relics.usage: All Operators start with +18 SP

relics.name: Vanilla Soda
relics.desc: Bolívar's cheap specialty drink dates back hundreds of years. It is mixed with a variety of spices, and is known for not being very good.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Auto Recovery skills by +0.2/s

relics.name: Fowlbeast Liver Pate
relics.desc: A small jar of fowlbeast liver pâté. Bright and vividly colorful, it is eaten only by nobles.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Auto Recovery skills by +0.35/s

relics.name: Dreaming Essence
relics.desc: Supposedly the Banshees make their aromatics only for their beloved. A single drop is enough to enchant just about anyone, bringing out any artist's inspiration and creativity.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Auto Recovery skills by +0.5/s

relics.name: Captain Morgan's Wine
relics.desc: A wine produced in the Iberian region. Despite having gained popularity in the heart of Victoria’s bustling cities, it is now discontinued.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Offensive and Defensive Recovery skills by 1 SP per 3s

relics.name: Water of Life
relics.desc: The strongest Ursine liquors go straight to one’s head, but it's said that the healthy Ursine adult can down several bottles.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Offensive and Defensive Recovery skills by 1 SP per 2.5s

relics.name: Royal Liqueur
relics.desc: The fine wines of the fallen nation of Gaul have now become prized treasures for collectors. The place of production has moved to Leithanien, but the technique has been lost.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Offensive and Defensive Recovery skills by 1 SP per 1.5s

relics.name: 'Unleashings'
relics.desc: After several attempts by Touch, nature briefly opens up to you.
relics.usage: All Operators recover 1 SP every time they take damage or Elemental Injury

relics.name: String Puppet
relics.desc: The more you struggle and try to break free, the tighter the strings get. Do you really think you are still thinking rationally?
relics.usage: Enemies receive 700 Arts Damage per second when stunned, frozen, or bound

relics.name: 'Children's Puppet'
relics.desc: A simple puppet made by Iberian children using trinkets found on the seacoast. Supposedly just holding it tight will give you much richer dreams.
relics.usage: Enemies receive 1000 Arts Damage per second when stunned, frozen, or bound

relics.name: Victory Horn
relics.desc: Blow the horn, cry out, and scatter our foes with its roar!
relics.usage: Increases the duration of single-instance status effects such as stun, cold, or freeze by 100%

relics.name: Flash Camera
relics.desc: Every photo shot by this camera is washed out in white, with only the victim's twisted expression faintly visible.
relics.usage: Increases the duration of single-instance status effects such as stun, cold, or freeze by 110%

relics.name: Therapy Tape
relics.desc: He tried to stop you from listening. He said it was dangerous. He said you should not stay. Do you understand now? You are listening to his song.
relics.usage: Increases the duration of single-instance status effects such as stun, cold, or freeze by 150%

relics.name: Royal Brooch
relics.desc: An ornament worn by Gaulish royalty, made from gold and inlaid with expensive Originium crystals. They are more than decoration: many of these brooches hide Originium circuits loaded with defensive Arts.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +15 RES, and dodge one instance of damage after deployment

relics.name: Old Fan
relics.desc: When it rains for several days on end, the old Yanese Tianshi complains to his friends about the pain in his knees, before casually waving his fan and driving away the rainclouds. It is about time he taught this trick to his young apprentice, currently working at the Central Judicial Office.
relics.usage: All Operators gain +10% ATK for every different type of Operator class in the squad

relics.name: Nachzehrer's Cane
relics.desc: Life is the Nachzehrer's sustenance and currency.
relics.usage: When a friendly unit is overhealed, nearby enemies take Arts damage equal to the overheal amount

relics.name: Angelina's Inspiration
relics.desc: The girl has no qualms about sharing her Originium Arts. She believes that you will use them for the right reasons.
relics.usage: All enemies have -15% Movement Speed, and -1 Weight Level

relics.name: Scout's Scope
relics.desc: Lock on, aim, fire, and destroy the enemy's will to resist, all in the name of a better future that Scout will never see. Maybe you can do it for him.
relics.usage: Enemies take increased damage from friendly units as range increases (max 100%)

relics.name: Black Tulip
relics.desc: The Silence crushed the Iberians, so she wishes to speak up. If her words are not sharp enough, then her sword will speak for her.
relics.usage: Friendly Operators gradually gain ATK when not using a skill, up to a maximum of +60% after 60 seconds; this bonus will be reset when a skill ends

relics.name: Assault Co-op - Sharp Blade
relics.desc: Use the sharpest spear to strike the hardest shield. I guarantee you that the shield will break first, trust me.
relics.usage: Increases the ATK of Vanguard and Guard Operators by +8% for each Vanguard or Guard Operator in the combat squad

relics.name: Fortification Co-op - Phalanx
relics.desc: Like a huge ship running over rubble.
relics.usage: Increases the ATK of Defender and Supporter Operators by +8% for each Defender or Supporter Operator in the combat squad

relics.name: Ranged Co-op - Remote Strike
relics.desc: If you can solve the problem from a reasonable distance, why do you have to literally butt heads?
relics.usage: Increases the ATK of Medic and Sniper Operators by +8% for each Medic or Sniper Operator in the combat squad

relics.name: Sabotage Co-op - Suppression
relics.desc: Don’t worry about accuracy. Take enough shots, and you’re guaranteed to land something!
relics.usage: Increases the ASPD of Caster and Specialist Operators by +6 for each Caster or Specialist Operator in the combat squad

relics.name: Spinach Pack
relics.desc: Many Cautus out there believe that popping the contents of this can into their mouths will grant them immense strength. The marketing's effectiveness is a classic success story in the industry.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +60% ATK for one second after their skill becomes active

relics.name: Wrath of Siracusans
relics.desc: The signature pasta dish of an 'authentic Siracusan restaurant' that once operated in the capital of Gaul. The dish's unique taste, and particularly its sauce, a blend of thick chocolate sauce and Originium Slug liver, earned the praise of many Gaulish nobles. Supposedly, though, this dish nearly caused a diplomatic crisis between Siracusa and Gaul.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +100% ATK for one second after their skill becomes active

relics.name: Ribbon of Honor
relics.desc: Numerous actors have played this character, yet none of them were able to save the character's dearest friend.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +100% ATK when blocking only one enemy

relics.name: Royal Alliance Treaty
relics.desc: The old Sarkaz courts sacrificed themselves to obtain their power. Ever since, the courts have come to terms with one another, banding together against their common enemy.
relics.usage: All Operators no longer receive any damage from Sarkaz enemies

relics.name: King's New Lance
relics.desc: Beautiful wishes made in dire straits can bring hope, even if they are not necessarily the truth.
relics.usage: When Life Point is 1, all friendly units have +50 ASPD

relics.name: Castle's Offspring
relics.desc: With enough time, this box will become a whole new stage.
relics.usage: All Operators gain +300 DEF and +30 RES after being deployed for 100 seconds

relics.name: Distant Home's Guide
relics.desc: This ancient ring is actually said to be a special navigation device, used to find emergency supplies that the Ægir once secretly stashed in various spots across the ocean. Follow the ocean's currents, be guided by wind; your homeland will always continue to bestow gifts upon you.
relics.usage: Designates one deployable tile at the beginning of battle; friendly units deployed on that tile will recover 1 SP and 3% Max HP per second

relics.name: Abandoned Banner
relics.desc: There is nothing special about this dismasted flag, but the Abyssal Hunters can use it to find combat supplies that the Ægir once secretly stashed in various spots across the ocean. They have no way of knowing what the ocean has forgiven, but there is no need for them to forgive. The fight never ends.
relics.usage: Designates one deployable tile at the beginning of battle; friendly units deployed on that tile have +50% ATK and +50 ASPD

relics.name: Medicine Sticks
relics.desc: In ancient times, whenever Great Yan's armies fought, they would march forward carrying these. Mulberry drew inspiration from this and made an edible plaster, excellent for working out the masseter muscle and revitalizing one's spirit.
relics.usage: All friendly units gain 2 layers of Shield when deployed

relics.name: Fincatcher's Shawl
relics.desc: A shawl often worn by Iberian seamen when they went to hunt giant fins in the shallows, the unique texture and color making them blend in with the reef. The Rhodes Island Engineering Department designed a combat uniform with an optical camouflage effect based on this prototype, which is currently in the testing stage.
relics.usage: Ranged Operators gain Camouflage

relics.name: 'Glorious Kazimierz'
relics.desc: A Kazimierzian best-seller that adapts the deeds of the arena's brightest-shining knights into a novel. Nearl, who was exiled but returned to reclaim her laurels, occupies a considerable portion of its pages.
relics.usage: Operators gain +70% ATK for 6 seconds after dodging

relics.name: 'The Return'
relics.desc: This Kazimierzian best-seller is said to be a collective creation by fans. 'Nearl' has already become an amalgamation of many ideals and fantasies. 'For her, exile was not an escape. She came back, and stood alone against the era's torrents.'
relics.usage: Operators gain +130% ATK for 6 seconds after dodging

relics.name: Convalescence Experience Card
relics.desc: A special service from the Convalescent Garden! Each Operator is entitled to one physiotherapy session from the Convalescent Garden before each of their field operations. Non-transferrable.
relics.usage: All Operators gain +40 ASPD for 10 seconds after deployment

relics.name: Convalescence Prime Membership
relics.desc: A special service collaboratively provided by the Convalescent Garden and the Medical Department! Each Operator is entitled to one comprehensive physical and psychological assessment, as well as a physiotherapy session. Afterwards, they can also taste Podenco's homemade flower cakes.
relics.usage: All Operators gain +70 ASPD for 10 seconds after deployment

relics.name: Old George Nutritional Paste
relics.desc: This poor-tasting fortified nutritional supplement was originally a 'dietary supplement' periodically provided to Columbian Pioneers, but after extensive marketing, it suddenly became a 'beauty product' beloved by nobles.
relics.usage: Operators have increased ATK proportional to how high their HP is, up to a maximum of +30% ATK at 100% HP

relics.name: Emergency Active Agent
relics.desc: An injection commonly used in Rim Billiton's local mines. Its strong active ingredient can stimulate the workers' senses and ensure their work efficiency in extreme environments. Life and engineering both have deadlines, and for them, this is simply a multiple-choice question.
relics.usage: Operators have increased ASPD proportional to how low their HP is, up to a maximum of +60 ASPD at 30% HP

relics.name: King's Buckler
relics.desc: Keep your eyes straight ahead, and let your stalwart heart resist all hardship.
relics.usage: When Life Point is at 1, all Operators have +2 Block

relics.name: King's Staff
relics.desc: Let go of worthless pomp and treat others with kindness. They will no doubt return in kind.
relics.usage: When Life Point is at 1, all Operators recover an additional 1 SP every 2 seconds

relics.name: King's Crown
relics.desc: O Nameless King, may you continue to bless Iberia, even should your kingdom fall to ruin.
relics.usage: When Life Point is at 1, all friendly units have +50% ATK, increased to +150% ATK if you have collected 3 or more King's Collectibles

relics.name: Timeworn Poetry Strips
relics.desc: 'Drunk far beyond all mounts that list the realm, all beings take on souls as they demand.'
relics.usage: Summons will no longer use Deployment slots

relics.name: Derivative Terminal - Supercharged Mod
relics.desc: A product of Raythean Industries. The latest remote-control operation terminal, capable of connecting to the signals of most existing remotely-operated units on its own, complete with customization options and performance upgrades. Not yet available on the market.
relics.usage: Summons have +30% HP, +30% ATK, and +30 ASPD

relics.name: Uncle Vought Total Care Kit
relics.desc: A complete care kit produced by the Columbian old salon product manufacturer, 'Uncle Vought.' Provence has modeled for this product. 'Take good care of your tail and your ears - after all, they're your second face.'
relics.usage: All friendly ranged units take 40% less Elemental Injury

relics.name: Ursus Felt Rug
relics.desc: The people of Ursus will spread this special felt rug across their cellars when storing grains for the winter, in order to absorb water and prevent moisture. When the cellar becomes a hiding place, it also helps shelter the occupants from the wind and snow for a while.
relics.usage: All friendly melee units take 40% less Elemental Injury

relics.name: Blunt Claws - Advancement
relics.desc: Plant a great stone within the waters. Split the waves and part the fog.
relics.usage: Vanguard Operators have -2 DP Cost and +60% HP

relics.name: Blunt Claws - Burst
relics.desc: He's already done all the reserve squad's work for them. What else do you expect?
relics.usage: Vanguard Operators start with +15 SP

relics.name: Blunt Claws - Proficiency
relics.desc: As an operator who often enters the battlefield before anyone else, you cannot fathom what they have been through.
relics.usage: The SP Cost of Vanguard Operators' skills is decreased by -35%

relics.name: Blunt Claws - Mastery
relics.desc: 'When the darkly-clad enemies rushed towards me, there were only four words on my mind — they are done for.'
relics.usage: Vanguard Operators have +50% ATK and DEF

relics.name: Bend Spears - Advancement
relics.desc: The warrior walks onto the battlefield. The warrior unsheathes his blade. The warrior faces his foe.
relics.usage: Guard Operators have -3 DP Cost and +40% HP

relics.name: Bend Spears - Acuity
relics.desc: 'This is the 7th blade you’ve broken this month.' 'But I cut down eight enemies this month.'
relics.usage: Guard Operators have +25% ATK

relics.name: Bend Spears - Bloodbath
relics.desc: Soldiers who get bolder as time passes are rare. Most need some form of psychological counseling to alleviate the trauma of war.
relics.usage: Guard Operators restore 2 SP after each attack

relics.name: Bend Spears - Army of One
relics.desc: One does not need to don heavy armor or hold up a shield to resist enemy attacks. Oftentimes, the best defense is to quickly eliminate those that would harm you.
relics.usage: Guard Operators have +1 Block

relics.name: Bend Spears - Deathmatch
relics.desc: I wouldn't really recommend learning those skills from Blaze.
relics.usage: Guard Operators have -40% DEF, but gain +40% ATK and +30 ASPD

relics.name: Iron Guard - Advancement
relics.desc: The shieldbearers formed a mountain range and became the earth. What they fight against are not enemies of flesh and blood. They fight against fate and injustice.
relics.usage: Defender Operators have -3 DP Cost and +40% HP

relics.name: Iron Guard - Invasion
relics.desc: Defensive actions for the sake of teammates or strategic purposes often make people forget about their original aggressiveness.
relics.usage: Defender Operators have +60% ATK

relics.name: Iron Guard - Advance
relics.desc: In exchange for an opportunity to attack, the shattered ranks made one more advance.
relics.usage: Defender Operators have -1 Block (will not be reduced below 1 on deployment), but gain +40% ATK and +40 ASPD

relics.name: Iron Guard - Impenetrable
relics.desc: Every blow they endure will turn into anger and be returned severalfold.
relics.usage: Defender Operators restore 2 SP after being attacked

relics.name: Iron Guard - Moving Fortress
relics.desc: Nobody can break through their defenses, and even fewer are able to escape from their assault. If every combatant has a mission, then that mission is to change the situation on the battlefield the moment they enter the fray.
relics.usage: Defender Operators have +100% ATK, +100% DEF, and +1 Block for 15 seconds after deployment

relics.name: Fatal Bolts - Advancement
relics.desc: Load, aim, and fire. Destruction arrives as scheduled.
relics.usage: Sniper Operators have -2 DP Cost and +60% HP

relics.name: Fatal Bolts - Precision
relics.desc: In an era when Originium Arts were not as robust as they are now, Yan had a saying, 'a pierced willow leaf from a hundred paces,' to praise others for their mastery of archery.
relics.usage: Sniper Operators have +20% ATK

relics.name: Fatal Bolts - Crossfire
relics.desc: The dense crossfire also exposes snipers to great danger. The next battle boils down to a game of vision.
relics.usage: Sniper Operators have -40% HP, but gain +40% ATK

relics.name: Fatal Bolts - Divine Speed
relics.desc: It is said that the legendary archers of ancient Victoria can almost shoot a continuous stream of arrows.
relics.usage: Sniper Operators have +70 ASPD

relics.name: Broken Wand - Advancement
relics.desc: Let Arts dance around your fingertips. Call the wind and rain, unravel reality, and achieve your grand goals.
relics.usage: Caster Operators have -3 DP Cost and +60% HP

relics.name: Broken Wand - Arts Weaving
relics.desc: When Leithanians gazes up at the great spires, they often see some strange or even celestial phenomena.
relics.usage: Caster Operators have +25% ATK and +25 ASPD

relics.name: Broken Wand - Concentration
relics.desc: 'When a caster intends to go all out, they will display a completely different temperament — even if we're talking about something like Skyfire's grumpiness.'
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Caster Operators by +0.4/s

relics.name: Broken Wand - Malediction
relics.desc: It is virtually impossible to identify when the Sarkaz first encountered Originium, and the origins of these ancient Arts have long been separated from actual reality and common sense.
relics.usage: Caster Operators have -40% HP, but deal +70% Arts damage

relics.name: Stalwart Aid - Advancement
relics.desc: Whenever you're 'almost done' with a task, what you need at that moment is someone to help you with the 'almost' part.
relics.usage: Supporter Operators have -2 DP Cost and +60% HP

relics.name: Stalwart Aid - Diligence
relics.desc: Operators tend to give it their all, even to tasks that might seem trivial. The reality is, those tasks are not trivial at all.
relics.usage: The SP Cost of Supporter Operators’ skills is decreased by -40%

relics.name: Stalwart Aid - Assistance
relics.desc: Bringing meals, showing the right route to take, or even having a heart-to-heart. The key difference between victory and defeat often lies in these little things.
relics.usage: Friendly units within the attack range of Supporter Operators gain +20% ATK

relics.name: Stalwart Aid - Counter-Arts
relics.desc: There are many ways to weaken an enemy's defensive capabilities: positional devices, armor-stripping Arts, marking weak points, and... giving them a stomachache.
relics.usage: Enemies within the attack range of Supporter Operators have -20% DEF and -20% RES

relics.name: Healer's Path - Advancement
relics.desc: The desire to save lives is a virtue that mankind must applaud, for that is the support that keeps us alive.
relics.usage: Medic Operators have -2 DP Cost and +60% HP

relics.name: Healer's Path - Self-Treating
relics.desc: Who says healers can't heal themselves? They simply pop some pills before continuing to help others.
relics.usage: Increases the SP regen rate of Medic Operators by +0.3/s

relics.name: Healer's Path - Keen Hands
