relics.desc: Compared to the feat of snatching a life from the grip of death, what is three days of coma afterwards?
relics.usage: Medic Operators have +50 ASPD

relics.name: Healer's Path - Restore Sanity
relics.desc: Making one's body as solid as a nomadic city is a bit of a romanticized description, but our medics are ready to put that idea into practice.
relics.usage: Friendly units within the attack range of Medic Operators gain Status Resistance

relics.name: Rusted Blade - Advancement
relics.desc: There may be many means, but only one end.
relics.usage: Specialist Operators have -2 DP Cost and +60% HP

relics.name: Rusted Blade - Execution
relics.desc: An endless amount of rust to scrape off, and endless amount of work.
relics.usage: Specialist Operators have +30 ASPD

relics.name: Rusted Blade - Isolation
relics.desc: The director of S.W.E.E.P. does not have any mission participation records. *None*.
relics.usage: Specialist Operators have +40% ATK and DEF

relics.name: Rusted Blade - No Man's Land
relics.desc: Sound is eliminated the moment the shadows pass by, and whoever hears them is next.
relics.usage: The Redeployment Time of Specialist Operators is reduced by -35%

relics.name: Rusted Blade - Overwhelm
relics.desc: Terrain is one aspect of battle... That’s all I can say.
relics.usage: Specialist Operators have +2 shift strength

relics.name: Hand of Spikes
relics.desc: Let the enemy make his move. He will be his own downfall.
relics.usage: Protector, Arts Protector, and Duelist Operators deal 100% DEF as Arts damage per second to all enemies they are blocking

relics.name: Hand of Choker
relics.desc: With your last strength, break open a last bit of hope for yourself.
relics.usage: Heavyshooter, Deadeye, and Besieger Operators' attacks will instantly defeat enemies below 20% HP

relics.name: Hand of Buckler
relics.desc: Drop by drop, bit by bit. It is unstoppable.
relics.usage: Decel Binder, Hexer, and Abjurer Operators' attacks deal additional Arts damage equal to 3% of the target's current HP

relics.name: Hand of Diffusion
relics.desc: Strength in numbers.
relics.usage: Fortress, Chain Caster, and Blast Caster Operators recover 2 SP each time they deal damage to an enemy unit

relics.name: Hand of Superspeed
relics.desc: You swing your hand, leaving not even an afterimage.
relics.usage: Executor, Ambusher, and Dollkeeper Operators gain +120 ASPD when there are no friendly units in the four adjacent tiles

relics.name: Hand of Snatcher
relics.desc: Whatever it is you do not have, snatch it away from your enemy. There is no need to hesitate.
relics.usage: Charger, Pioneer, and Tactician Operators' DP Cost -12 and gain 6 additional DP after defeating an enemy

relics.name: Hand of Purification
relics.desc: Where it is, dust is not.
relics.usage: Bard, Phalanx Caster, and Therapist Operators deal 50% ATK as Arts damage to all enemies in their attack range

relics.name: Hand of Pulverization
relics.desc: Your goal isn't just to hit the bullseye over and over.
relics.usage: Artilleryman, Splash Caster, and Flinger Operators gain +15% ATK for every time they deal damage to the same unit, up to 150%; this bonus will reset if no damage is dealt for 5 seconds

relics.name: Hand of Flowing Water
relics.desc: The ocean's flow never ceases, because it is always surging.
relics.usage: Increases the SP recovery rate of Core Caster, Mystic Caster, and Summoner Operators by +0.5/s whenever they use a skill, up to 4 stacks

relics.name: Game Room Admin Access Card
relics.desc: With this, you have become master over Rhodes Island's entertainment privileges. Please exercise caution when processing the various strange applications from the Operators... and remember to lock the door every day.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote an Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Miss.Christine Petting Ticket
relics.desc: You have the lady's permission!
relics.usage: Immediately Promote 2 Operators (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Antique Casting
relics.desc: 'Heavenly forge, take the bounty of the earth.' ...Even though it can be used for all kinds of work, nobody understands its exact mechanism.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote 3 Operators (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Topographical Map
relics.desc: A hand-drawn map that exudes a sense of antiquity. The labels and corresponding terrain have been enlarged, and there seems to be something off about its scale.
relics.usage: +1 Deployment Limit, all friendly units gain +4% DEF

relics.name: Conch of Divine Whispers
relics.desc: Sound it, and gods you've never seen or even heard of will answer your call. Actually, it's just a hollow conch shell.
relics.usage: +1 Deployment Limit, all friendly units gain +4% HP

relics.name: Night-Whispers in the Forest
relics.desc: A special aromatherapy set prepared for field operators by the Convalescent Garden, consisting of fifteen herbal extracts in 172 different combinations. When the wind wends into wooded vales, all creatures lower their voices, and one can find respite.
relics.usage: +2 Deployment Limit, all friendly units gain +6% DEF

relics.name: Church Alms Meal Voucher
relics.desc: In the declining coastal towns of Iberia, certain churches will provide alms in the form of meals, which their believers can redeem using vouchers. As for how these vouchers are distributed, that is a secret.
relics.usage: +2 Deployment Limit, all friendly units gain +6% HP

relics.name: Regional Action Plan
relics.desc: Rhodes Island has dedicated offices in every major hub. The Messengers are doing what they can to weave a fragile web across the world.
relics.usage: 4-star Operators cost -2 Hope to recruit

relics.name: Comprehensive Operation File
relics.desc: A vessel sailing upon the land is like treading upon thin ice. Remember to always expand your worldview.
relics.usage: 5-star Operators cost -2 Hope to recruit

relics.name: Secret HR Letter
relics.desc: '■■■ gave it to you? Never mind, then.'
relics.usage: 6-star Operators cost -2 Hope to recruit

relics.name: Draft of a Speech
relics.desc: She was a little nervous, a little worried, but still remained resolute. When the microphone turned on, her long ears quivered slightly.
relics.usage: All Operators cost -2 Hope to recruit

relics.name: Rusted Iron Hammer
relics.desc: 'Change is necessary, and we must do all we can to hasten the oncoming storm.'
relics.usage: Shop vendors charge -50% Originium Ingots for their goods

relics.name: Rhodes Island Tactical Transceiver
relics.desc: Hang in there, help's on the way.
relics.usage: Gain an additional choice when Recruitment Vouchers are dropped after battle

relics.name: Four-Leaf Clover Fossil
relics.desc: A symbol of luck that Magallan found in a place all but devoid of life. The rock has no doubt experienced many violent changes of the lands, but it survived, giving those who come across it a small bit of lingering hope.
relics.usage: Gain an additional choice when loot drops from a Wish Fulfilled node or after battle

relics.name: Acahuallan Bowl
relics.desc: Fruit is one of the locals' staples, often eaten fresh right off the tree.
relics.usage: Restore 4 Life Point, +2 Objective Shield

relics.name: Three-Dimensional Art Display
relics.desc: The impression that Rhine Lab often gives is akin to this work of art: inscrutable, multi-faceted, impossible to sift fact from fiction.
relics.usage: Restore 7 Life Point, restore an additional 1 Life Point after each battle

relics.name: 'Multipurpose' Hair Clip
relics.desc: Its materials are clearly damage-resistant, but are its other functions as impressive as what the Kazimierzian merchant boasted? Come to think of it, why does a bobby pin need so many extra features anyway?
relics.usage: +1 Key, +1 Die Roll, +1 Objective Shield

relics.name: Three-Key Contract
relics.desc: According to regulations, lighthouse keepers must always carry three keys: one to unlock the main door, another to establish communication, and one to activate the searchlight. Sign your name, and the contract is set in stone. Carry the keys with you at all times, regardless of life or death.
relics.usage: +3 Keys

relics.name: Portable Scriptures
relics.desc: The few Iberian faithfuls who can still read often carry around a pocket-sized version of the Scriptures with them, taking it out to glance over a few lines during the brief lulls in their struggles for survival. It offers but a modicum of peace.
relics.usage: +2 Die Rolls

relics.name: Inquisitor's Scripture Cloth
relics.desc: As part of the decorations adorning an Inquisitor's clothing, this ribbon is inlaid with the Scriptures. Their hearts are firm and unwavering, and these accessories reinforce that image.
relics.usage: Gain +1 Die Roll after each battle

relics.name: Deep-Sea Sire Sculpture
relics.desc: The Church of the Deep cultists believe that gods sleep in the depths of the ocean, and the teeming Sea Terrors seem to confirm their beliefs. Seeing as that is indeed the truth, they no longer have any reason to hesitate.
relics.usage: +6 Die Rolls

relics.name: Duck Lord's Golden Brick
relics.desc:
'How can someone completely rid themselves of misfortune?' 
'You have a gold brick in your hand, don't you? Shove it up misfortune's mouth.'
relics.usage: Upgrades your die

relics.name: Defender-2
relics.desc: A mass-produced defensive-type drone. It doesn't make sense how it only seems to be useful in the hands of the enemy.
relics.usage: +5 Objective Shield

relics.name: Embedded Armor Plate
relics.desc: 'You don't understand a thing, test subject. Compared to what those monsters of Ursus suffered, a measly plate of armor embedded into your body isn't even worth mentioning.'
relics.usage: -2 Max Life Point, +8 Objective Shield

relics.name: Dario's Lantern
relics.desc: A lantern once wielded by Inquisitor Dario. Even after his death, the flames of his conviction still burn brightly upon this land.
relics.usage: When obtained, suppresses a designated Operator's Rejection

relics.name: 'King's Fellowship'
relics.desc: The Cultists of the Deep did not achieve success overnight; they lingered at the junction between land and sea for ages, preparing for the calamity that would come to be known as the Profound Silence.
relics.usage: +2 Objective Shield, coverts all Life Point into an equivalent amount of Objective Shield (Cannot be reduced below 1 by this effect)

relics.name: Tulip's Secret Formula
relics.desc: An emergency topical ointment produced through empirical analysis and numerous clinical trials, capable of effectively inhibiting the adverse side-effects Seaborn cells cause within the human body.
relics.usage: When obtained, suppresses a designated Operator's Rejection

relics.name: Paraffin and Balsam
relics.desc: Burn it and your impurities shall be shunned, allowing you to remain a pure human being. Praise the flame.
relics.usage: -30 Light, when obtained, suppresses a designated Operator's Rejection

relics.name: Nightsun Grass
relics.desc: Only a faint glimmer of light remains on the leaves of the nightsun flower. Contains certain detoxifying compounds and can be used as medicine.
relics.usage: +5 Light

relics.name: Cathedral Puzzle
relics.desc: Every time a piece of the puzzle falls, the Iberian Cathedral becomes more complete. In these times of decay, there is ample comfort to be found in simply being able to piece together one of history's most glorious wonders.
relics.usage: When obtained, randomly gain 1 Revelation

relics.name: Coin-Operated Toy
relics.desc: No idea why anyone would like a design like this, but its overall concept is indeed praiseworthy—Just insert a coin, and even a statue will start moving. There is nothing truer in this world.
relics.usage: For each 5 Originium Ingots in possession, all friendly units have +3 ASPD

relics.name: Chivalric Commandments - New Edition
relics.desc: Taking solemn vows is a thing of the past. The spirit of modern knighthood requires more flexible displays. Go ahead and input your number in the communications device, and you too will be an honorary knight.
relics.usage: For each 5 Originium Ingots in possession, all friendly units have +5 ASPD

relics.name: Portrait of Breogan
relics.desc: The subject of the portrait is an Ægir who used to be well-known in Iberia. His inventions and feats once made the country shine, but when the tide drowned out the golden light, his fate was sealed.
relics.usage: All Operators lose 15 SP when deployed

relics.name: Leviathan's Anabolism
relics.desc: Slowly did He fall into the deepest depths of the ocean. His blood, flesh, skin, and bones separated and dissolved—they were swept up by the sea's currents, absorbed by all life, and became sustenance for the propagation of the entire ocean.
relics.usage: All enemies gain +25% HP

relics.name: Failed Specimen
relics.desc: An incomplete shelled specimen. Debris falls from small crevices, sticks together, accumulates, and merges with the dead plants inside, until the original husk is completely swallowed up.
relics.usage: When obtained, immediately afflicts a random Operator with a Rejection

relics.name: 'Emergency Light'
relics.desc: Ægir warriors used to lock some kind of small luminescent Sea Terror inside a transparent contraption to serve as lighting during emergency situations. The restless Sea Terror would smash into its confines, releasing even more light in a frenzy until it died, staining its prison with an indescribable black glue.
relics.usage: Lose an additional 2 Light after each battle

relics.name: 'Luminous Corpse'
relics.desc: To provide light to we many during long migrations, the weakest organs among the collective would dissolve on their own and start glowing, leaving nothing behind but a transparent membrane.
relics.usage: Lose an additional 3 Light after each battle

relics.name: Bishop's Research
relics.desc: Almost all of the bishop's lifelong research can be found among these documents and notes, many of which involve experiments that transcended ethics and morality. In his eyes, making contact with the Seaborn was just a means to an end for his ultimate vision: allowing human beings to evolve into a more powerful existence through fusion with the Seaborn.
relics.usage: Lose an additional 5 Light after each battle

relics.name: Caerula Animus
relics.desc: The surviving core organ of the 'Firstborn' that devoured Its birthplace and soon died afterwards. Only the Seaborn are able to assimilate it, but only humans are able to understand the grief contained within. Alas, even for those whose evolution never ends, their - Their - suffering will always persist.
relics.usage: -50 Light; when obtained, immediately afflicts a random Operator with a Rejection, and leads the exploration toward a different ending

relics.name: Breath of the Tide
relics.desc: He appeared near you because he smelled the scent of the tides. You mean nothing to him.
relics.usage: +1 Deployment Limit; when obtained, [Tide-Hunt Knight] will appear on the battlefield until defeated

relics.name: Regressed Rocinante
relics.desc: Only a hero can ride it. When the hero dies, so too does its life end.
relics.usage: [Tide-Hunt Knight] will no longer appear on the battlefield

relics.name: 'Absurd Fate'
relics.desc: His name is widely known in Kazimierz, and his descendants set up companies to benefit from his renown. All the things he defended with his life have become nothing more than gold coins in a wallet.
relics.usage: +10 Originium Ingots, +1 Key

relics.name: The Knight's Corpus
relics.desc: Resisting does not come without a price. The knight's body had long since become riddled with wounds, but he paid no heed. He will fight until the bitter end, even should his body be smashed to pieces.
relics.usage: [Tide-Hunt Knight] has -80% DEF

relics.name: 'Last Refrain'
relics.desc:
Cry, cry, for where are the Abyssal Hunters now? 
Alas, alas, there is nothing in the ocean but the sound of the tide.
relics.usage: When obtained, randomly gain 2 Revelations

relics.name: 'Civilight Eterna'
relics.desc: A small, black crown.
relics.usage: All enemies take +150% True damage

relics.name: 'End of Times'
relics.desc: As long as a new cycle has not been reached, there is still time to fix your mistakes.
relics.usage: For one time only, if you would fail any battle other than the final battle of the area or a Leader battle, you instead gain +1 Life Point and advance to the next step instead of concluding the exploration

relics.name: 'Flames of the Inquisition'
relics.desc: Because the evil creatures were born from the sea, they were afraid. Afraid of the flames that would never mix with the sea.
relics.usage: Operators recover 2 SP when dealing damage to [Sea Monsters]

relics.name: 'Painful Happiness'
relics.desc: Oripathy is spreading among the troupe members. Some collapsed, some broke out crying, some were overjoyed, and some turned their attention to writing. It was at such a time that the young man received his coming-of-age present. He will sing his very last song as an actor.
relics.usage: All enemy units have -15% HP and Movement Speed

relics.name: 'Little Gran Faro'
relics.desc: The illumination device produced after reverse engineering the Eye of Iberia. It is powerful enough to penetrate layers of dark clouds, as well as temporarily repel powerful enemies.
relics.usage: Deals Stun on all enemy units for 5 seconds every time an enemy unit enters the Protection Objective

relics.name: 'Memories of The Shard'
relics.desc: 'It has pierced Victoria's heart, and is watching the empire bleed.'
relics.usage: Deals 3000 True Damage on all enemy units every time an enemy unit enters the Protection Objective

relics.name: 'Glory Pack'
relics.desc: A special betting method set up by the organizers for the duration of the knight competition season. Choose from a variety of complementary betting packages to minimize your losses on any particular match.
relics.usage: When an Operator is defeated, it will Stun all enemies in the four adjacent tiles for 6 seconds

relics.name: 'Sui's Wrath'
relics.desc: The Feranmut lifts its head, heaven and earth cower in fear.
relics.usage: Whenever you deploy a unit, deal 3000 Arts damage to a random enemy and the area around it

relics.name: 'Bedrock'
relics.desc: A weapon said to have been made specifically for an Abyssal Hunter. His master once fought alongside Skadi, smashing the tentacles of a benthic behemoth. But when he cast aside his weapon to block a bony projectile for Skadi, he vanished into the midst of the Sea Terrors' swarm.
relics.usage: All Operators have +5 DP Cost, but gain +15% ATK, DEF, and HP

relics.name: 'Wavebreaker'
relics.desc: A weapon said to have been made specifically for an Abyssal Hunter. Her master once was one of Gladiia's team members, responsible for culling countless Sea Terrors. But in order to allow the other teams to approach the benthic behemoth, she voluntarily stayed behind after being seriously wounded, and nobody ever saw her again.
relics.usage: All Operators have -25% HP, but have -50% Redeployment Time

relics.name: 'Pulse of the Ocean'
relics.desc: An exquisite water flow meter, capable of monitoring the flow of ocean currents in real time. A common sight among the Ægir people's underwater transportation tools.
relics.usage: All friendly units have +10 ASPD, all enemy units have -10 ASPD

relics.name: 'Determination'
relics.desc: Mizuki's humanity has not faded. He has accepted the Seaborn as a human, and he can transform back from a Seaborn to a human.
relics.usage: All Operators gain Block +2, DEF and HP +120%, and RES +20; leads the exploration towards a different conclusion

relics.name: 'Vigil'
relics.desc: Mizuki does not particularly identify with either humans or Seaborn. He only does what he thinks is right.
relics.usage: All Operators gain Block +1; leads the exploration towards a different conclusion

relics.name: 'Hesitation'
relics.desc: The manifestation of Mizuki's self-doubt, as his Seaborn instincts overcome his humanity. It will continue to have a negative impact, as long as his self-identity remains unclear.
relics.usage: All Operators gain Block -1 (will not go below 1); leads the exploration towards a different conclusion

relics.name: 'Endless Life'
relics.desc: Sac-like tissues found in Sea Terrors, used to store eggs prior to laying them. They detach from the host after death, maintaining activity for a period of time. Be careful not to break them!
relics.usage: Afflicts a random Operator with a Rejection at the start of each new floor

relics.name: Caerula Memory
relics.desc: Even vague memories of the lab's research are enough to induce fear. Perhaps you and people like you have all done something unforgivable for the sake of survival.
relics.usage: Light -100, immediately afflicts a random Operator with a Rejection

relics.name: Caerula Arbor
relics.desc: Through His body, Mizuki becomes one of Them. His consciousness is long gone, but His cells live on. The magma is His food, and the great tree grows towards the core of the planet. If left unchecked, He will become reborn in the planet's core, and invite Terra into we many.
relics.usage: Enemy ASPD +15; at the end of operation, if no Life Point is lost, gain +15 Light

relics.name: HR Bronze Seal
relics.desc: Emblem assigned to specific Operators when ■■■ receives an emergency deployment command. Your receipt of it signifies that ■■■ has completed the assignment.
relics.usage: Recruit a random 5-star Operator that is automatically Promoted

relics.name: Doctor Silver Seal
relics.desc: Emblem that you instructed to be sent out at the right time before you left. Your selected Operator has joined the field mission at the camp, while handing it back to you the way you left it.
relics.usage: Recruit a random 6-star Operator that is automatically Promoted

relics.name: Wordless Coral
relics.desc: There is no writing on it, only a faint scent. Chemicals are transmitted through the air, communicating information to we many.
relics.usage: Recover 5 Light for every die roll

relics.name: Underground Scorch
relics.desc: The people of the sea are unconcerned with what happens on land, and thus will never understand the warmth of the sun which reaches into the soul.
relics.usage: At the end of operation, if no Life Point is lost, gain +6 Light

relics.name: Great Machinations
relics.desc: 'Do not envy the sun on the surface. We can do better.'
relics.usage: At the end of operation, if no Life Point is lost, gain +9 Light

relics.name: Solidified Lamp Oil
relics.desc: Solidified oil that can be added to a lamp to keep it burning bright.
relics.usage: Light loss from battle damage reduced by 40%

relics.name: Sanctified Lamp Oil
relics.desc: Processed lamp oil that the Inquisition issues to its Inquisitors. The scent sharpens the mind, keeping them alert and aware of every evil.
relics.usage: Light loss from battle damage reduced by 60%

relics.name: 'King's Crystal'
relics.desc: The Iberians' contact with the Seaborn goes back further than they can imagine, but they did not know what the Seaborn were back then.
relics.usage: At the end of battle, -2 Life Points (will not go lower than 1), +1 Hope, +5 Originium Ingots

relics.name: Hand of Branding
relics.desc: Victory is the best supply.
relics.usage: Whenever a [Dreadnought], [Duelist], or [Soloblade] defeats an enemy, all Operators of these classes gain 1% ATK, 1 ASPD and 1% max HP permanently (max 99)

relics.name: Wrankwood Safety Suit
relics.desc: Used at Wrankwood studios for high-difficulty shoots. Inspired by military gear. FEater once refused to wear it while shooting a collaborative project.
relics.usage: For each Cursed Curio you have, all allied units gain DEF +25%, RES +10

relics.name: 'Flashing Swords'
relics.desc: Limited-edition montage from Wrankwood, showing the making-of and behind-the-scenes footage of classic action movies.
relics.usage: For each Cursed Curio you have, all allied units gain ASPD +35

relics.name: Mercenary Insurance
relics.desc: Special insurance offered to independent mercenaries, indemnifying against mission failure or excessive loss.
relics.usage: All friendly units gain 10% Sanctuary, increased to 35% if Light is below 50.

relics.name: Broken Wand - Learning
relics.desc: When you have taken in the wisdom of all the land, ignorance becomes the enemy's weakness.
relics.usage: For every collectible owned, [Caster] ATK +2%, ASPD +1

relics.name: Broken Wand - Light of Wisdom
relics.desc: Knowledge and experience should not remain untapped. They should be the key to victory.
relics.usage: For every collectible owned, [Caster] ATK +3%, ASPD +2

relics.name: Broken Wand - Solving
relics.desc: A caster's greatest enemy is another caster.
relics.usage: For every [Caster] Operator on the field, enemy RES -12

relics.name: Iron Guard - Wall
relics.desc: No one is stupid enough to ram a wall with their body, right?
relics.usage: [Defender] Operators gain DEF +40%, RES +10

relics.name: Iron Guard - Tower
relics.desc: It rises into the clouds, looking down at the enemies jumping up and down by its feet.
relics.usage: [Defender] Operators gain DEF +70%, RES +20, Elemental Injury taken -60%

relics.name: Survivor Contract
relics.desc: A very long contract, in which every term and condition is the result of experience, leaving no room for chance or sentiment. Satisfy them all to be the final survivor.
relics.usage: At the start of battle, a random Operator gains +20% ATK and DEF, plus an additional +20% at the end of each battle

relics.name: Fosterer Genotype
relics.desc: Collective evolution is driven by individual mutation. Individual problems are solved by collective intelligence. Lu is highly confident in this model. If the Fosterer Project has enough time, perhaps...
relics.usage: An Operator with Rejection will assimilate 2 nearby normal Operators. When assimilation is complete, the Operator with Rejection temporarily gains [Evolution]: Rejection has no effect, gains 35% Sanctuary, attacks ignore 35% RES and DEF

tools.name: Support Replenishment Station
tools.desc: An emergency Portable Supply Station provided to field operators.
tools.usage: Bring a Portable Supply Station along to each battle

tools.name: Support Landmine Set
tools.desc: An emergency set of Interference Mines provided to field operators.
tools.usage: Bring 3 Interference Mines along to each battle

tools.name: Support Mr. Boom
tools.desc: An emergency Mr. Boom provided to field operators.
tools.usage: Bring 2 Mr. Booms along to each battle

tools.name: Support Escape Crane
tools.desc: An emergency Snowsant's Safe Escape Crane provided to field operators.
tools.usage: Bring 2 Snowsant's Safe Escape Cranes along to each battle

tools.name: Support Riot Pile
tools.desc: An emergency set of Riot Piles provided to field operators.
tools.usage: Bring 2 Riot Piles along to each battle

tools.name: Support First-aid Kit
tools.desc: An emergency First-aid Kit provided to field operators.
tools.usage: Bring 3 First-aid Kits to each battle

bands.name: Leader Squad
bands.usage: +2 Max Life Point, restore 1 additional Life Point after each battle

bands.name: Support Squad
bands.usage: Start with +20 Originium Ingots and +2 Hope

bands.name: Spearhead Squad
bands.usage: Life Point is set to 1; All Operators gain +15% ATK and +15% HP

bands.name: Tactical Assault Squad
bands.usage: [Guard] and [Vanguard] Operators cost 2 less Hope to recruit, 1 less Hope to promote, and have a greatly increased chance of being randomly promoted

bands.name: Tactical Fortification Squad
bands.usage: [Defender] and [Supporter] Operators cost 2 less Hope to recruit, 1 less Hope to promote, and have a greatly increased chance of being randomly promoted

bands.name: Tactical Ranged Squad
bands.usage: [Medic] and [Sniper] Operators cost 2 less Hope to recruit, 1 less Hope to promote, and have a greatly increased chance of being randomly promoted

bands.name: Tactical Destruction Squad
bands.usage: [Caster] and [Specialist] Operators cost 2 less Hope to recruit, 1 less Hope to promote, and have a greatly increased chance of being randomly promoted

bands.name: Research Squad
bands.usage: Start with +3 Objective Shield; +30% Command EXP gained from battles

bands.name: First-Class Squad
bands.usage: When Recruiting for the first time, gain a random Advanced Recruitment Voucher

bands.name: Mind Over Matter Squad
bands.usage: Battles have a higher chance to yield another collectible; you may choose an additional reward after completing a commission; start with 'Survivor Contract'

bands.name: Resourceful Squad
bands.usage: Gain 2 Die Rolls upon entering a new floor; your starting die is upgraded to an 8-sided die; more dice available in battle

bands.name: People-Oriented Squad
bands.usage: All Operators cost -1 Hope to recruit and promote; you may choose an additional reward at the 'Safe House' node; max Command Level +1

variations.name: Exploration
variations.func: This floor has 2 additional nodes, but twice as much Light will be lost

variations.name: Predation
variations.func: On this floor, all your units and non-Boss units gain +50 ASPD, and your units receive -70% healing effectiveness

variations.name: Assimilation
variations.func: On this floor, you will obtain 3 Originium Ingots every time you enter a non-combat node, but will lose all Originium Ingots gained on this floor after exiting

variations.name: Death and Rebirth
variations.func: Battles on this floor drop +100% Originium Ingots; upon entering a combat node, you will immediately lose 1 Life Point (Cannot drop below 1 from this effect)

variations.name: Proliferation
variations.func: Battles on this floor have a 30% chance to drop a Recruitment Voucher; all enemy units have +15 RES

variations.name: One Among Many
variations.func: Battles on this floor grant +50% Command EXP; all enemy units have +50% DEF

variations.name: Overgrowth
variations.func: After the end of each battle on this floor, gain 1 Max Life Point; however, Life Point and Shield losses are doubled

variations.name: Adaptation
variations.func: The first Wish Fulfilled node you enter on this floor will grant 1 random collectible, but you will no longer be able to identify node types beyond the nodes directly ahead of you

endings.name: Precious Days
endings.desc: In the end, the goal envisioned from the very beginning is brought to fruition, and the two creatures, from Seaborn to human, are ready to usher in a new tomorrow after a full meal. Everyday life is the greatest joy that fate bestows upon the living, and indeed, peaceful days are the most joyous of all.
endings.change: The ocean has no more knights, and Mizuki is able to return to his journey.
cond: Default ending. Clear the floor 5 Dreadful Foe 「Curse of Cognition」 while holding none of 「Caerula Animus」, 「'Determination'」, 「'Vigil'」, or 「'Hesitation'」.

endings.name: Age of the Silence
endings.desc: In silence the terrible waves rise, and within it disappears the knight's howl. The calamity that once destroyed Iberia surges again, and this time it shall not recede.
endings.change: The ocean is the enemy of the knight, and it has already soaked its way into your clothes.
cond: First achieve the ending 「Precious Days」 on Surging Waves difficulty or higher to unlock the related events and collectibles.

「Delusions of Lunacy」: found before the Dreadful Foe at the end of floor 3. Accept the knight's help to obtain the collectible 「Breath of the Tide」 — from then on, 「파도를 사냥하는 기사」 joins battles on non-hidden floors (until defeated).
(Optional) 「The Knight Persists」: can be met before reaching floor 5 while holding 「Breath of the Tide」.

Keep 「파도를 사냥하는 기사」 alive until you reach floor 5. On entering floor 5, the Dreadful Foe becomes 「'Fate's Favored Child'」 and the final boss becomes 「The Last Knight」. If the knight dies, this ending is permanently lost for the run.
Achieved by clearing floor 5 while holding none of the four collectibles above.

endings.name: Price of Peace
endings.desc: The conflict over the 'Firstborn' comes to an end, and the human Him triumphs over the inhuman Him. The Silence fades, the tides retreat, and the being known as Mizuki shall never again return to the land.
endings.change: Undercurrents surge beneath the tide; the blood of the 'Firstborn' leads Skadi back to We Many, and the Silence draws near.
cond: First achieve the ending 「Age of the Silence」 on Surging Waves difficulty or higher.
「The Beginning」: before the Dreadful Foe at the end of floor 3, choose to dive into the deep sea to obtain the collectible 「Bishop's Research」 (Light drains automatically each battle).

「Caerula Animus」: reach the floor 5 Dreadful Foe with 「Bishop's Research」 and at least 20 Light, then choose 'Search the garden' to obtain 「Caerula Animus」 (large Light loss + one Operator becomes hostile).
(Optional) 「Paean」: can be met before the floor 5 Dreadful Foe while holding 「Caerula Animus」.
Clear floor 5 while holding 「Caerula Animus」 to enter floor 6, then clear the final boss 「Destiny of We Many」 (Ishar'mla, the Corrupting Heart).

endings.name: Stella Caerula
endings.desc: The descendants of evolution rise from Terra's tides, break through the skies, and fly towards the endless sea of stars. There is no more pain, no more suffering, only the joy of a new life.
endings.change: The age of Man is over. The age of the Seaborn begins.
cond: After Expansion II, achieve the ending 「Age of the Silence」 on Surging Waves difficulty or higher to unlock the event 「The Beginning」.
「The Beginning」: before the Dreadful Foe at the end of floor 3, choose '"The deep sea"?!' to obtain 「Caerula Memory」 (Light -100, one random Operator becomes hostile).
(Optional) 「Caerula Ritual」: can be met before the floor 5 Dreadful Foe while holding 「Caerula Memory」.

「Sublimation」: appears before the floor 4–5 Dreadful Foe while holding 「Caerula Memory」. Grants one of 「'Determination'」, 「'Vigil'」, or 「'Hesitation'」 — 「'Vigil'」 requires 50+ Light and 「'Determination'」 requires 90+.
Clear floor 5 while holding any of the three to enter floor 6, then clear the final boss 「Glory of Humanity」 (Ishu'mka, the Fountain of Life).

encounters.title: Neither Black Nor White
encounters.desc: You rescue an Iberian man who was entrapped by Sea Terrors, and he thanks you for your kindness and gratefully offers to repay you. Judging from the style of his robes, he appears to be an Iberian priest. However, you can't be entirely sure... since the local Church of the Deep cultists also wear similar attire.

choices.title: Gladly accept
choices.desc: You have a chance to obtain a random Collectible

next.desc: He affectionately calls you his friend and draws two boxes from his robes, beseeching you to choose one as your thank-you gift. Be it from the insignias on his clothing or the engravings on the boxes, you clearly recognize him as a member of the Inquisition.

choices.title: Don't ask for anything in return

next.desc: How can you accept a gift simply for being in the right place to lend a helping hand? You wave your hand at the priest and immediately turn to leave; he stands there staring at your back in a daze, not uttering a word.

choices.title: Choose the box with a sword and gun
choices.desc: Obtain a random Collectible that may be combat-oriented

choices.title: Choose the box with a lantern
choices.desc: Obtain a random Collectible that may be adventure-oriented

choices.title: Choose the box with hands clasped in prayer
choices.desc: Obtain a random Cursed Curio, but gain 3 Die Rolls

choices.title: Choose the box with interlocking keys
choices.desc: Obtain a random Cursed Curio, but gain 2 Keys

encounters.title: Ocean's Legacy
encounters.desc: Before the Profound Silence came, the high seas used to teem with tales of the pirates: they plundered the King's vessels and sailed circles around the Grand Armada. They looted all in their wake, and were sung of in legends as being even wealthier than entire countries, yet sometimes also stealing from the rich to give to the poor. But after the Profound Silence, these tales abruptly came to an end, along with Iberia's Golden Age. Now, you're convinced you've located a trove of pirate treasure, but can't help but be alarmed by cornucopia of jewels and baubles overflowing all around you.

choices.title: Plunder now, worry later
choices.desc: Obtain a random Collectible and a random Cursed Curio

encounters.title: The Last Tidewatcher
encounters.desc: A lone Ægir stands against the tide. The turbid waves coalesce into high walls and come crashing down on the cliff beneath his feet again and again, nearly swallowing it several times over. However, the man does not retreat even half a step. The waves carry away two pieces of equipment at his feet, and you recognize them—one is a 'Verticis Navis,' a short-range submersible, and the other is an 'Ocean Voyager,' a device used to mark territory—two instruments frequently used by Ægir tidewatchers.

choices.title: Take the risk to help him recover his equipment
choices.desc: Lose 1 Life Point, but randomly obtain a mysterious Collectible

choices.title: Ask him about what he's seen

next.desc: The wind and the waves have calmed, meaning that the next round of tides is coming. The tidewatcher has no intention of talking to you, urging you to leave as soon as possible to avoid danger.

encounters.title: Singer by the Sea
encounters.desc: A solitary Sea Terror sings its dying elegy as it lies on the reef, its tentacles flicking away at a human musical instrument. The discordant notes of the harp and the euphonious melody of its song resound together between heaven and earth.

choices.title: Stop and listen
choices.desc: Lose 3 Originium Ingots, but randomly obtain a mysterious Collectible

choices.title: Turn and leave
choices.desc: Don't overthink things; just cover your ears

next.desc: Though singing can invariably tug at a person's heartstrings, these are not the sounds of a human being, and it is ultimately better to avert your ears.

encounters.title: Traditional Technology
encounters.desc: While exploring a cave, you run into an old man who is maintaining two weapons far too large for him. 'I am a craftsman, and I'm preparing weapons for my Hunters.' He pauses for a while, before continuing: 'Ægir no longer has any Hunters; it only has me, a useless old man.' He clenches his fist tightly, using this to cover up his inner rage. 'You're also a warrior, right? Then take one of these weapons, and treat it as a token of our meeting.'

choices.title: Opt to choose neither
choices.desc: These weapons are hard to lug around

next.desc: 'True... True... How could a landwalker ever wield a Hunter's weapon? Begone, stranger, and never come back.'

encounters.title: The Wares Peddler
encounters.desc: An Ægir is showing you an exquisite instrument. According to him, it is capable of monitoring ocean currents to infer changes in marine life, worthy of its status as one of Ægir's secret treasures. 'I've been stuck here for years with nobody to lead us back home. Ægir... no longer exists. He's heard that the most powerful country on land is called Victoria, and he plans to use the money from selling treasures to buy himself some supplies and start a new life there.

choices.title: Purchase the instrument
choices.desc: Lose all Originium Ingots, but obtain the Collectible

choices.title: Try out the instrument
choices.desc: Lose 5 Life Point, but obtain the Collectible

choices.title: Sounds a little sketchy...
choices.desc: Can't rely on someone like this

next.desc: A member of your party interrupts your conversation to remind you that his description is greatly exaggerated, and that this instrument may not be all that special in Ægir.

encounters.title: Ignorance is Bliss
encounters.desc: You find a scientific research facility in a cave deep in the ocean. Most of the devices here have been damaged, and you can't discern their functions at all. Only one terminal can be booted up.

next.desc: After being supplied with power, the terminal just barely flickers to life, its existing power supply not estimated to last long. Better start reading through the interesting files as soon as possible.

choices.title: Maybe next time
choices.desc: Originium Ingots don't grow on trees

next.desc: You have no way of interacting with these so-called projects at all. Maybe the researchers here weren't able to make any significant progress before the facility was shut down.

A few lines of text pop up on the terminal: 'Project: Survival. Objective: Collection of nutrients, efficient allocation of resources. Permission to use this matrix has been granted to relevant personnel.'
A few lines of text pop up on the terminal: 'Project: Migration. Objective: Expansion of territory, seizing of resources. Permission to use this matrix has been granted to relevant personnel.'
A few lines of text pop up on the terminal: 'Project: Reproduction. Objective: Efficient propagation, consumption of resources. Permission to use this matrix has been granted to relevant personnel.'

A few lines of text pop up on the terminal: 'Project: Growth. Objective: Provide nutrition, produce resources. Permission to use this matrix has been granted to relevant personnel.' However, after a long wait, what appears in front of your eyes is a picture book. Inside are hand-drawn depictions of dozens of marine animals and plants that seem familiar to you. Other than this, you don't find any useful materials.

choices.title: It looks pretty, but unfortunately doesn't seem useful

encounters.title: Path of Suffering
encounters.desc: Under the Inquisition's orders, certain sacraments of penance have already been merged with heavy manual labor. Everyone who completes the ritual will be rewarded, but honestly speaking... how can anyone pass a trial like this?

choices.title: Start by moving the food
choices.desc: Lose 2 Life Point, but gain 1 Hope

choices.title: Toil in the fields
choices.desc: Lose 2 Life Point, but gain 1 Max Life Point

choices.title: Ring the giant bell
choices.desc: Lose 2 Life Point, but gain 1 Key

choices.title: Transport building materials and fuel to the watchtower
choices.desc: Lose 2 Life Point, but receive a random Collectible

next.desc: You don't have that much confidence in your physical abilities. Maybe some other time.
You don't have that much confidence in your physical abilities. Maybe some other time.

encounters.title: Empathy
encounters.desc: You sneak into a settlement of Church of the Deep cultists where Sea Terrors and humans seem to be coexisting in a delicate equilibrium. It seems that as long as you provide them enough food and don't show any signs of aggression, they aren't so scary after all. But... you already know without even having to think about it—the very act of feeding a Sea Terror... is extremely dangerous.

choices.title: It'd be best to leave now

next.desc: Perhaps an Inquisitor will soon come to deal with this settlement, but you don't think you'll ever forget this astounding place.

choices.title: Try feeding them again?
choices.desc: Lose 2 Life Point, but the chance to successfully feed is reduced to 50%

choices.title: Feed the nethersea brand by the pool
Feed the Retching Broodmother
choices.desc: Recruit one Operator (from a selection of 2 random classes)

encounters.title: Faith
encounters.desc: An armory collapsed following an attack from Church of the Deep cultists, and the Inquisition has begun to survey the damage after repelling the enemy. Due to the extent of it, they are willing to sell you some of the salvaged materials to offset the loss.

choices.title: Bust out the wallet
choices.desc: Lose 6 Originium Ingots to dig around for some goodies

choices.title: I'll contribute in order to exterminate the Seaborn
I'll contribute in order to gain the wealth in front of me
Luck's not on your side; grab what you can

next.desc: Money ought to be spent wisely.

choices.title: I'll contribute in order to find the truth behind this incident
I'll contribute in order to protect the people of Iberia

encounters.title: Booty Bay
encounters.desc: You search around for the riches buried here, but the treasure has been concealed by vegetation and soil. How are you supposed to get at it?

next.desc: After hearing you sound the horn, the awakened 'locals' come out to 'warmly welcome' your arrival. Surely they will be willing to help you find the treasure after you defeat them.
You decide not to go looking for trouble. Safety first, after all.

encounters.title: Dancing Chests
encounters.desc: A group of Sarkaz are singing and dancing around various treasure chests, several of which are opening and closing along with the dance. The Sarkaz don't seem hostile, and you might even be able to join in for a bit of fun and relaxation. One of the dancers twirls and pirouettes to your side, trying to drag you into their dance.

next.desc: The dancer pulls you close to the pile of treasure chests, and you suddenly see a Sea Terror mimicking the shape of a gold coin inside one. It suddenly dawns upon you that this group of Sarkaz is most likely with the Church of the Deep! When you turn around, they've already drawn their weapons and are glaring at you maliciously.

You dodge the dancer's outstretched hands and simply watch the Sarkaz dance from a distance. You don't want to delve into dumb questions about why there are Sarkaz in Iberia; you simply want to watch other people dance and relax before continuing onward to face the hardships that lie ahead.

encounters.title: A Faint Spark
encounters.desc: The devouring dust has already blanketed the entire city, digesting the products of civilization in an orderly manner to turn them into sustenance for the Seaborn's progeny and evolution. You trudge through the dissolving ruins, searching for any supplies that might still be usable. Unintentionally, your attention is drawn to something glowing from beneath a patch of dust.

choices.title: Take the risk to go retrieve it
choices.desc: Lose 3 Life Point, but obtain the Collectible

choices.title: Scavenge for supplies from a safe area

encounters.title: Lingering Glimmer
encounters.desc: You see an Inquisitor who has lost her lantern. She gasps for breath as she leans against her sword, a mountain of Sea Terror corpses at her feet.

choices.title: Quietly approach
choices.desc: Lose 2 Life Point, but gain 15 Light

encounters.title: Delusions of Lunacy
encounters.desc: The flames in the sky burn wantonly, turning into a faint residual warmth when they land on the knight's shoulderguards. He goads his mount and gallops atop the waves. He gazes out, and there is nothing but pure azure all around him, the sound of rushing waves ever incessant. The ears of wheat are calling.

choices.title: Rage
choices.desc: Lose 2 Hope and Max Hope, but obtain the Collectible

choices.title: Call out
choices.desc: Lose 1 Die Roll for a chance to gain the knight's assistance

choices.title: Leave
choices.desc: What exactly is he trying to do?

encounters.title: The Knight Persists
encounters.desc: A windless day, the ocean's surface as serene as a mirror. The knight smashes the mirror underfoot and looks at his reflection, seeing only an illusion of himself treading across the sky, eyes cast down upon the masses. The knight lifts his lance to thrust, and his fake points his weapon at the sea. Suddenly, the ocean caves in and a storm rushes forth, the sea and sky joining forces to kill the knight. Civilization despises him, yet nature also rejects him. The name of his enemy, is Terra.

choices.title: Resist
choices.desc: Lose 2 Life Point, but obtain the Collectible

choices.title: Pass by
choices.desc: Maybe I should keep my distance from this lunatic...

encounters.title: The Beginning
encounters.desc: The world is undergoing drastic changes. The news terminals will not stop beeping, and bad news continues to spill forth: the Sea Terrors are marching towards land, the Penal Battalion's fleet has been destroyed, and the Church of the Deep cultists have advanced on the Bastión de Cánticos. However, what worries you most is news of the Hunters who entered the Stultifera Navis. Mizuki found documents detailing plans to lure the Abyssal Hunters back into the ocean to resurrect Him. And another document, titled 'Caerula,' records a bishop's lifelong research on the Seaborn, as well as a certain location.

choices.title: Decide to dive into the abyss

encounters.title: Caerula Animus
encounters.desc: A gargantuan corpse lies at the location marked at the map. A large colony of Sea Terrors gathers around it, devouring its forever-growing remains. Mizuki leads you around them, towards the inside of the corpse. After fumbling around in the darkness for a long time, the two of you finally find traces of civilization. A dust-sealed laboratory long lost to time awaits its guests. Mizuki cannot open the heavy, worn doors, but the surveillance equipment unlocks them after recognizing your faces. Since the Bishop of the Deep was convinced that this was the source of the Seaborn, this primordial power can surely help you save the Abyssal Hunters. You and Mizuki begin to search the laboratory.

choices.title: Search the research area
choices.desc: Lose 20 Light, but obtain a random Collectible

choices.title: Search the storage room
choices.desc: Lose 20 Light, but gain 13 Originium Ingots

choices.title: Search the living quarters
choices.desc: Lose 20 Light, but gain 5 Die Rolls

choices.title: Search the cafeteria
choices.desc: Lose 20 Light, but gain 6 Max Life Point

encounters.title: Paean
encounters.desc: You find an area next to His lair that is untouched by the Seaborn. There is nothing inside but ocean sand, a broken figure of a man, and a pair of sleeping beauties still clutching their weapons.

choices.title: Kiss the Hunters' foreheads and take their artifacts

encounters.title: Caerula Ritual
encounters.desc: You witness Mizuki go into the transportation pod at the bottom of the experimental hub. You go to the control station to investigate the experiment that Mizuki is about to face. It is a long, fully-automated modification process that will take centuries, perhaps millennia, but the control station does not display any concrete numbers. Even more concerning is His assimilation of the experiment hub. You can see the branch-like tentacles reach out and retract, absorbing energy from the magma, and it has fully assimilated part of the experimental hub. Now, you can choose to turn off part of the experiment process to ensure Mizuki's safety, or let him achieve a further understanding of He through the assimilated structure.

encounters.title: Sublimation
encounters.desc: The collective seeks survival, and survival is suffering. Therefore, we continue to evolve, overcoming flaws, facing nature, fighting external foes. Extinction is cunning and hungry, and it will tear us apart at the slightest sign of weakness. Therefore, we have adapted and addressed our shortcomings. From emerging out of the seas, from contacting Originium, we have expanded our territory and come into contact with deadly threats, until we fully understand, until they become food. Now, the land and sea are our paradise, but we continue to grow. Life remains suffering. We need a bigger home. Before we welcome the evolutionary singularity, He who leads our future must give Himself an answer: Who am I?

choices.title: 'I am Mizuki. I will fulfill the Doctor's request.'
'We many is not me. I am we many.'

encounters.title: Gathering Stormclouds
encounters.desc: You've never seen such dense storm clouds before. The tempest forces its way across the ocean at an alarming rate, the Originium dust swept up in its vortex spinning violently as if intent on destroying everything, smashing the thick shells covering the ocean to pieces. You stare blankly at the storm raging in front of you, all the Catastrophe-monitoring instruments having failed. In the face of nature's onslaught, what can living creatures hope to achieve?
encounters.note: Can appear more than once in the same run

encounters.title: Cliffside Burial
encounters.desc: You are standing in a cliffside cemetery, where a farewell ceremony is being held. The people of the sea will burn the body, let the sea wind carry away the ashes, then erect a coffin at that spot to serve as a tombstone for mourning. You vaguely recall that the ceremony of these people used to be burial out at sea, but this one is clearly a rejection of the ocean. The cognitive dissonance leaves you somewhat puzzled, but you still stop for a while to offer your prayers to those who have passed on.

choices.title: Stop to pray
choices.desc: Gain 1 Die Roll and 4 Originium Ingots

encounters.note: Can appear more than once in the same run

encounters.title: Buy Now, Print Now
encounters.desc: You find a high-tech box the size of a coffee table sitting along the coast. After you give all its buttons a good press, it spits out a pamphlet with a catalogue of its various products. Apparently, this is some kind of lotto machine that floated here from Ægir. It appears to support six or seven different payment methods, but due to environmental constraints, you are only able to use the most primitive one: sticking in coins.

next.desc: The internal mechanisms whirr over and over, making various mechanical sounds, and the machine soon returns to its original state. You now know that this device is an amazing printer of sorts, and all its prizes are printed right here on the spot.

The machine seems to detect that you are about to leave, and spits out a coin at you. Seeing this, you go back and forth several times to nab a few coins from the machine. By the time you try for your fifth coin, you find that the machine has already blacklisted you.

choices.title: Why not toss in a few more coins?

encounters.title: Meeting in a Dream
encounters.desc: The overflowing dream-mists whisk you away from reality and usher you into a paradise beneath the sea, where your kin stand on the rusty hull of a sunken ship. They seem to be communicating about something, and you are surprised to discover that they seem to be individual entities rather than parts of a single collective. You cannot help but approach them, wanting to observe them in greater detail.

choices.title: Discern their vocalizations
choices.desc: Lose 10 Originium Ingots, but gain 4 Max Life Point

next.desc: Water drips onto the stalagmites. Water drips onto your face. You finally wake up.

choices.title: Discern their vocalizations again
choices.desc: Lose 10 Originium Ingots, but gain 4 Max Life Point

choices.title: Discern the quality of their voices again
choices.desc: Lose 10 Originium Ingots, but gain 4 Hope

choices.title: Carefully summarize their speech patterns
choices.desc: Lose 10 Originium Ingots, but gain 1 Key

choices.title: Record what you hear again
choices.desc: Lose 4 Hope and Max Hope, but gain 3 Max Life Point

choices.title: Carefully summarize what you hear
choices.desc: Lose 4 Hope and Max Hope, but gain 2 Die Rolls

choices.title: Observe their appearances
choices.desc: Lose 4 Hope and Max Hope, but gain 4 Objective Shield

choices.title: Attentively summarize their appearances
choices.desc: Lose 3 Life Point, but gain 1 Die Roll

choices.title: Get a bit closer again
choices.desc: Lose 3 Life Point, but gain 2 Hope

choices.title: Try hard to join in on their conversation
choices.desc: Lose 3 Life Point, but gain 3 Objective Shield

choices.title: Discern the quality of their voices
choices.desc: Lose 10 Originium Ingots, but gain 4 Hope

next.desc: Water drips onto the stalagmites. Water drips onto your face. You finally wake up.

encounters.title: Emergency Dispatch
encounters.desc: A batch of support supplies have arrived at the nearest safe house. You need to decide whether to have Logistics transfer them over, or to send over your operators who are familiar with the area.

encounters.title: Inheritance
encounters.desc: There is a dead Seaborn by the shore. Though its body is shriveled, it seems to be clutching tightly onto something, unwilling to let go.

choices.title: Retrieve what the Seaborn was protecting

encounters.title: Devouring Dust
encounters.desc: The coast in front of you has already turned an ashen white, and when the sea wind blows, the debris of the earth dissipates along with it. Where soil seems to blur into dust, everything seems to be slowly burning away. You want to go take a closer look, but a man suddenly grabs you and hands you a protective suit. 'Put this on if you don't want to die.'

choices.title: Hurry up and put on protective clothing

encounters.note: Can appear more than once in the same run

encounters.title: Camp
encounters.desc: You find a quiet corner safe enough to harbor your entire squad from harm, allowing you to rest and reorganize. After you help your teammates get settled in, your nerves start to unwind as well.
encounters.note: Can appear more than once in the same run

encounters.title: Catastrophe Messenger
encounters.desc: A Victorian-born Catastrophe Messenger is setting up beacon points high up along the coastline and adjusting the logging equipment. You happen to bump into him while he's fetching some water to drink, and he invites you for a cup of tea at his makeshift camp.

choices.title: Give the Messenger a gift
choices.desc: Lose 6 Originium Ingots, but gain 2 Keys

encounters.note: Can appear more than once in the same run

encounters.title: King No More
encounters.desc: You find an empty Iberian city, devoid of people and with no signs of Sea Terrors. In the middle of the city stands a magnificent museum. You study the nearly completely worn-away leaflets and discover that the museum was dedicated to the unsung kings of Iberia.

choices.title: Search for the king's treasure
choices.desc: You have a chance to obtain a King's Collectible

choices.title: Continue searching for the king's treasure
choices.desc: Lose 1 Die Roll for a chance to obtain a King's Collectible

next.desc: After some digging around, you come up empty-handed, and have to head back with only the tattered leaflet as your bounty. These kings are far too removed from the current era: their names, titles, coats of arms, and corresponding artifacts all lost to history. All the Iberian people can do is to treat them as a collective whole, reciting the same few words over and over about how the ancient past shapes the present.

encounters.title: Medic's Will
encounters.desc: The coastline devours the land, and civilization decays along with it. Wisdom gives way to thaumaturgy, and ignorance shambles across the land. You meet a wandering Iberian medic on his own, picking herbs for his patients. He sees that you are 'well armed' and begs you to keep him safe. You agree.

choices.title: Chat with the wandering medic
choices.desc: Obtain a Book of Scriptures and a random Collectible, but it seems like you'll run into trouble later on

encounters.title: Heart of a Fool
encounters.desc: Inland, you meet the guards of one of the nearby villages. Their village is wracked with an affliction, and their holy collectibles were stolen for no reason. As a passing traveler, as long as you are able to solve their problems, you will forever be a friend to the village.

next.desc: The guards tense up as soon as they see the object you just produced. Their captain tells you that this is one of the old saint's relics, the spiritual backbone of the village—and it would collapse without it. It was stolen a few days ago, and they had just caught the thief—the same person you met earlier. He sizes you up, then looks back at the tied-up wandering medic, before motioning for the guards to surround you.

choices.title: Take out the nightsun grass
choices.desc: Show the guard the wandering medic's medicinal herbs

next.desc: You take out some of the medicinal herbs the wandering medic gave you and teach the guards how to apply them. You watch their faces turn to shock, befuddlement, then sudden realization, before their captain hurriedly tells his men to release the captured wandering medic. He regretfully admits that he had mistakenly arrested him under suspicion of being a thief from the Church of the Deep who stole the village's collectibles, and now assures you that he will keep him safe. You are also entitled to the gratitude and repayment of the village, and you decide to ask for––

encounters.title: Homecoming
encounters.desc: You run into the priest while looking for the Messenger station. He claims to have been away from Iberia for a long time, pursuing truth all across the land, but is now returning to offer some assistance to his homeland. He is willing to help you contact local operators, but also emphasizes that the road ahead is tough, and all he can do is make contact.

choices.title: Entertain him
choices.desc: Lose 2 Originium Ingots, but recruit one Operator (from a selection of 2 random classes)

choices.title: Make a large contribution
choices.desc: Lose 5 Originium Ingots, but recruit any Operator

encounters.title: Like Flames to Wood
encounters.desc: While collecting firewood, you discover a strange forest. The trees here have no leaves, and their bark is thin and transparent, as smooth as skin. When you scratch one with a knife, a strangely-scented resin flows forth. You chop a few branches before heading back. At night, the bonfire in the darkness attracts many lost travelers, and you take them in. Everyone says that as you were the one to bring this flame, you needn't worry about the night vigil.

choices.title: Sleep soundly
choices.desc: Recruit one Operator (from a selection of 2 random classes)

choices.title: Maintain a rotating night shift
choices.desc: Lose 10 Light, but obtain a Recruitment Voucher

encounters.title: Safe House
encounters.desc: The resources that Rhodes Island has in Iberia prohibit it from building safe houses as comprehensive as those in other regions, but functionally, there is no difference between them. For the time being, the cold and the damp are staved off, the flames dance, and fresh food steams. Rest well, and prepare yourself, for greater waves are still ahead.

next.desc: The resources that Rhodes Island has in Iberia prohibit it from building safe houses as comprehensive as those in other regions, but functionally, there is no difference between them. For the time being, the cold and the damp are staved off, the flames dance, and fresh food steams. Rest well, and prepare yourself, for greater waves are still ahead.

encounters.title: Mutual Aid
encounters.desc: An Ægir rescues you from being swarmed by Sea Terrors and takes you to a secluded sanctuary. Heavy rain falls outside the window, and you see no signs of fire anywhere along the entire horizon. The sole source of light comes from the faint lantern in the Ægir's hand. His sanctuary seems to have a lot of equipment left behind by the Inquisition. He notices that these items have piqued your interest, and suggests he'd be willing to exchange them for the supplies you've gathered outside.

choices.title: Agree to make a deal
choices.desc: Let's see what you guys have

encounters.title: Worship the Flame
encounters.desc: You see a group of Iberian priests carrying around a huge furnace with a writhing and shrieking Sea Terror nailed to the inside. The head priest laments to you that all this destruction was wrought by the Seaborn, and claims that his congregation has found a weapon capable of slaying them all. All that's required is the flame—the Seaborn will have nowhere to run, and people afflicted by the Seaborn will be able to regain their original forms. You listen to his fervent preaching before turning your eyes to the Sea Terror that has ceased its struggling, an uneasiness welling up in your chest.

choices.title: Pray to the flames for your suffering operators
Flames cannot save this land
choices.desc: Seems like there's going to be trouble

next.desc: You tell these fanatics that flames cannot completely destroy the Seaborn. The only thing this execution-by-immolation accomplishes is to make the Seaborn adapt faster and understand fire sooner. The priests are extremely dissatisfied by your words—they denounce you as a Church of the Deep cultist, and wish to throw you into the furnace for a trial by fire.

encounters.title: Lonely Elder
encounters.desc: You pass an old Iberian man, and can vaguely hear his entourage addressing him as 'Saint' in hushed voices. He stops you, full of suspicion as he questions you. Where did you come from? What are you doing? You answer his questions calmly and with grace, and his tone gradually softens. You return his questions, and though your eyes are on him, his gaze is fixed on the ocean behind you... 'Finally, someone has come to put an end to it.' It's about time, he says.

choices.title: Talk to him
choices.desc: The old man sizes up your equipment

next.desc: The old man provides you with a map of the local area, marks the places where you can obtain supplies, and tells you that foreigners best not linger here for too long.

choices.title: Talk to him
choices.desc: The old man furrows his brow

next.desc: Your lighting equipment has broken, and the night is fast approaching. The old man hesitates for a moment, then passes you the lantern in his hand. 'This was originally a collectible left behind by an old friend. Instead of letting it sink into the ocean with me, it ought to remain here on the land.'

choices.title: Accept his help
choices.desc: Gain 5 Originium Ingots, 2 Max Life Point, and 2 Objective Shield

next.desc: You turn down the old man's goodwill, but solemnly thank him nevertheless.

encounters.title: Puppy-Dog Eyes
encounters.desc: A Perro is quietly tailing your squad, seemingly trying to make contact with you. But when you try to get close to him, he just hides far, far away. You have no idea what exactly he's trying to do, but the sound of his sobbing won't stop ringing in your ears. It's maddening.

next.desc: In any case, this Perro has to go. Right now, this instant, immediately!
Your group picks up the pace and takes a few detours, and eventually, the irritating sobbing sound fades away.

encounters.title: Duckforce Test
encounters.desc: Civilization here is on the decline, and even a mountain of gold can hardly buy you a few supplies... Hold on a sec, is that Duck Lord? And he's trying to do business with Church of the Deep cultists?!

next.desc: Your enemy's friend is also your enemy, and this duck has stepped out of line.

choices.title: Why does it matter to me?

next.desc: As long as these cultists don't go out of their way to attack you, there's no need to get in the way of their deal.

encounters.title: Some Time to Kill
encounters.desc: While your team is resting, you run into Duck Lord who is yet again in the middle of business negotiations. His bodyguard, Gopnik, is humming an Ursine ditty to himself. Maybe you can find something for him to do?

choices.title: Loosen up your muscles?
choices.desc: Let's treat this like a good warm-up

next.desc: You raise your fists, and Gopnik starts walking towards you waving his fists as well.

choices.title: Don't make a fool of yourself

next.desc: After Duck Lord finishes his business, Gopnik leaves with him.

encounters.title: A Passion for Work?
encounters.desc: That Perro showed up again, except this time, he's standing in front of you as if trying hard to tell you something. Unfortunately, he stands there hesitating for what must be eons, not uttering a single word. You let out a sigh, and go up to grab the piece of paper from his hand yourself... Alright, let's take a look... It's a self-rights and responsibilities contract... from Duck Lord?

choices.title: Who wants to work for that goddamn duck?!
choices.desc: Is there no limit to your pranks?!

next.desc: You have every reason to suspect that the guy in front of you is messing with you, and the damn sobbing makes you even angrier.

choices.title: Who wants to work for that goddamn duck...

next.desc: You sigh, make up some platitudes to fool him with, and send him on his way. It's not easy to find a job these days, let alone for such a taciturn person.

encounters.title: Business Empire?
encounters.desc: Duck Lord appears in front of you with his new employee, and he wants you to leave this place for a simple reason—his 'business partner' takes issue with you, and your presence is preventing him from making money.

next.desc: This duck's really pushing his luck. Time to teach him a little lesson.
After seeing you acquiesce to his demands, Duck Lord swaggers away with his underlings in tow.

encounters.title: Tulip's Commission
encounters.desc: You switch on the communication terminal and find that Tulip has sent you three messages. Judging from their titles, they seem to be three urgent commissions with different degrees of difficulty. As Rhodes Island's branch leader in Iberia, she needs to make full use of the resources at her disposal to ensure the survival of the Infected. Even if you are busy with other important matters, she will still issue these commissions to you after careful deliberation.

choices.title: Take a look at the trickiest commission
Take a look at the mid-tier commission
Take a look at the simplest commission

next.desc: You are so focused on the problems in front of you that you don't have the time to take on additional commissions.

encounters.title: At a Glance
encounters.desc: Fragments of the forest of monoliths are being assimilated by the nethersea brand, sinking next to the withered giant. Their inscriptions constantly change, showing a myriad of possibilities: You become the partner of a woman you know, or you become her mortal enemy; you lead mankind to fight the Seaborn, or you lead the Seaborn as one of their own; you stand on Terra to gaze at the stars, or you stand on ■■■ to gaze at Terra. You want to keep investigating, but Mizuki motions for you to stop for now. The nethersea brand is constantly analyzing information. What you have read, we many will also know.

choices.title: Try to cleanse the nethersea brand
Maybe you can take care of some problems for your 'species'

encounters.title: Chance Meeting
encounters.desc: You accidentally barge into a cave while trying to avoid the storm's sudden onset, where you find a gravely wounded Sea Terror. No, rather... it is an imperfect splicing between man and Sea Terror, with fragments of a priest's uniform still hanging from its body. The moment it sees you, a gleam of light flashes within its dying eyes.

choices.title: Check how it's doing
choices.desc: Lose 3 Life Point, but gain 8 Originium Ingots

choices.title: Try to save it
choices.desc: Lose 6 Life Point, but gain 4 Max Life Point

choices.title: Quietly back away
choices.desc: Maybe it's a trap by the Church of the Deep cultists

next.desc: You have little interest in monsters that are neither human nor Sea Terror. However, it will attract other Sea Terrors, so it is best to get out of here as soon as possible.

encounters.title: Overseas Export
encounters.desc: You see an RV on the side of the road. A Columbian from the vehicle is currently hawking beer while scraping the logos off the bottles.

choices.title: Buy a few to sample the taste
choices.desc: Lose 5 Originium Ingots, but gain 3 Hope

next.desc: You describe to them the dangers of Iberia and suggest that they take their business to another country. The Columbian thanks you before driving off in a hurry.

encounters.title: Seaborn Scholar
encounters.desc: An old Ægir is currently expounding to you on how to deal with the Seaborn. According to him, he used to specialize in Seaborn behavior at a scientific academy, but the academy was destroyed by the Seaborn, and he was swept away from home by the ocean currents. Fortunately, there are also people here who are fighting the Seaborn, so his research can still be put to good use. Now, he has made equipment from the carcasses of some Sea Terrors he collected, and hopes you can use it to gather more data for his experiment.

choices.title: Why not give it a try?
choices.desc: Obtain a random Collectible, but it probably isn't anything good

next.desc: If his research was so useful, his city wouldn't have been destroyed, so you refuse to be his test subject.

encounters.title: For Survival
encounters.desc: After you earn the trust of the local sea people, they take you into a labyrinthine cave. The place winds deep underground and is filled with giant shelled creatures you've never seen before. The sea people are nervously at work prying open the shells of these creatures; as soon as they do so, the packages of flesh within spit out a large amount of secretions that are quickly collected, filtered, and packaged. Nobody says a word, trying to keep their motions as minimal and efficient as possible, keeping everything in order as if working in a secret factory.

choices.title: The pay's great, so join in
It's an easy job
choices.desc: Lose 3 Originium Ingots, with a 70% chance to gain 8 Originium Ingots

choices.title: Keep working
choices.desc: Lose 10 Originium Ingots, with a 50% chance to gain 25 Originium Ingots

next.desc: During a work break, you hear the sea people talking amongst themselves in hushed voices—another group of their companions responsible for bringing back the giant shelled creatures failed to return. This is a necessary sacrifice; everyone understands this in their heart, but they will not stop. There will always be those who have to pay the price so that more may survive. As for how long this method can continue, nobody can say for sure. After you depart, the sea people must continue to slink around in hopes of survival, but you have more important things to do.

encounters.title: Advance Squad
encounters.desc: You locate the Rhodes Island squad that sent out the original distress signal. Its members have been scouting the surrounding areas before you made contact. You exchange information, and they share some of their resources and experience with you.

encounters.title: Lighthouse Keeper
encounters.desc: An old man drew a lighthouse in the sand, but the surging tide quickly erases all traces of it. 'The ocean really swallowed them all up this time,' he mutters to himself. You strike up a conversation with him, who as you might imagine was formerly one of Iberia's many lighthouse keepers. You find out that he came here for a reunion with old friends. 'Were they all lighthouse keepers as well?' 'They are now.' Before you leave, the old man hands you some specialties as a parting gift.

encounters.title: Bygone Customs
encounters.desc: Back in the days when the fincatching boats still sailed, children in coastal Iberian villages would make a wish for their parents who went out to sea to return with a gift. When the adults returned, they would personally hand over the largest finbeast to their children, its belly stuffed with whatever they wished for, or some other surprise. Apparently, children in wealthier families could make more than one wish, but now, all that remains before you are a few dried finbeasts with wish strips attached.

choices.title: Take the fin with a wish strip attached to it.
choices.desc: You have a chance to obtain this Collectible

choices.title: Take the fin with a wish strip attached to it from under the table.
choices.desc: Resourceful: Using your knowledge of Iberian customs, you have a chance to obtain this Collectible

items.name: Hemopoietic Inhibition
items.usage: Operators with this Rejection will continuously lose HP whenever their HP is not full
items.desc: Accelerated blood cell apoptosis, inhibited bone marrow hematopoiesis

items.name: Concentration Disorder
items.usage: Operators with this Rejection will automatically activate their skill
items.desc: Impairment of sensory functions, loss of coordination, loss of control over stress mechanisms

items.name: Neurodegeneration
items.usage: Operators with this Rejection will be Frozen for 15 seconds after deployment (Effect only triggers once before unit leaves the field)
items.desc: Atrophy of neurons in the brain, delayed reactions, dissociated consciousness

items.name: Metastatic Aberration
items.usage: Operators with this Rejection have -50% Max HP, ATK, DEF, Deployment Cost, and Redeployment Time
items.desc: External cells observed in circulatory system, ectopic electrical signaling, whole-body tissue spasms

items.name: Hemopoietic Inhibition+
items.usage: Operators lose HP at a faster rate when their HP is not full, but gain increased ATK based on how low their HP is
items.desc: Accelerated blood cell apoptosis, inhibited bone marrow hematopoiesis

items.name: Concentration Disorder+
items.usage: All Operators will automatically activate their skills, and while no skill is active, have gradually increased ATK and gradually decreased ASPD; this effect resets when the skill duration ends
items.desc: Impairment of sensory functions, loss of coordination, loss of control over stress mechanisms

items.name: Neurodegeneration+
items.usage: Operators will be Frozen for 30s after deployment, but gain +200% ATK and DEF for the duration (Effect only triggers once before unit leaves the field)
items.desc: Atrophy of neurons in the brain, delayed reactions, dissociated consciousness

items.name: Metastatic Aberration+
items.usage: Operators have -75% Max HP, ATK, DEF, Deployment Cost, and Redeployment Time, but start with +80 SP
items.desc: External cells observed in circulatory system, ectopic electrical signaling, whole-body tissue spasms

items.name: Degeneration and Disorder
items.usage: Operators with this Rejection will automatically activate their skills and be Frozen for 15 seconds after deployment
items.desc: Complete cognitive degeneration, loss of self-control.

items.name: Degeneration and Inhibition
items.usage: Operators with this Rejection will continuously lose HP whenever their HP is not full and be Frozen for 15 seconds after deployment
items.desc: Internal structural obstruction, thoughts fail to translate into actions.

items.name: Degeneration and Aberration
items.usage: Operators with this Rejection have -50% Max HP, ATK, DEF, Deployment Cost, and Redeployment Time. They will also be Frozen for 15 seconds after deployment
items.desc: Irreversible physical aging, highly susceptible to external damage.

items.name: Disorder and Inhibition
items.usage: Operators with this Rejection will automatically activate their skills and continuously lose HP whenever their HP is not full

items.name: Disorder and Aberration
items.usage: Operators with this Rejection will automatically activate their skills, and have -50% Max HP, ATK, DEF, Deployment Cost, and Redeployment Time
items.desc: Systemic disarray, heightened discomfort, difficulty in movement.

items.name: Inhibition and Aberration
items.usage: Operators with this Rejection continuously lose HP whenever their HP is not full, and have -50% Max HP, ATK, DEF, Deployment Cost, and Redeployment Time
items.desc: Physical atrophy, inability to react to environmental changes.

items.name: Evolution
items.usage: Rejection has no effect, gains 35% Sanctuary, attacks ignore 35% RES and DEF
items.desc: Operator with Rejection has gained Evolution

items.name: 6-Sided Die
items.usage: A commonly-seen six-sided die. Seems to be able to decide on certain things after being cast.

items.name: 8-Sided Die
items.usage: An uncommon eight-sided die. Seems to be able to decide on certain things after being cast.

items.name: 12-Sided Die
items.usage: An extremely rare twelve-sided die. Seems to be able to decide on certain things after being cast.
